okf_archive.overview: The Archive Filter allows you to process different types of files stored in a ZIP archive file (e.g. .zip or .jar files). It extracts files matching specified name patterns from the archive and processes them using designated Okapi filter configurations.

identification.description: File identification settings that control which files within the archive are extracted and which filters process them.

identification.mimeType.description: The MIME type for the format of the archive file itself (not the files contained within it). This identifies the container format.

identification.fileNames.description: One or more file names or patterns to match against files inside the archive. If several file names are listed they must be comma-delimited. You can use file wild-chars (? for single character, * for multiple characters). Files matching these patterns (in any directory inside the ZIP file) will be extracted for processing. There should be as many file names as filter configuration identifiers in the configIds field, with entries in the same order.
notes: You should have as many file names as filter configuration identifiers in the configIds field.

dependsOn.condition: must have the same number of entries, in the same order

identification.configIds.description: One or more filter configuration identifiers that specify which Okapi filter to use for processing each matched file. There must be as many configuration IDs as there are file names in the fileNames field, and they must be in the same order. For example, 'okf_properties' would use the Properties Filter with its default configuration.

dependsOn.condition: must have the same number of entries, in the same order

inlineCodes.description: Inline code detection and handling settings. When enabled, allows pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.) within the extracted translatable text.

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: The filter takes an archive document (e.g. a ZIP file) as input and extracts any files matching the specified name or pattern, processing each with the filter specified by the corresponding filter configuration identifier.
Files are matched in any directory inside the ZIP file — the match applies to the file name only, not the full path within the archive.

examples.title: Extract properties files from a ZIP archive
examples.description: Configure the filter to extract all files named Res.properties from anywhere inside the ZIP file and process them using the Properties Filter with its default configuration.
examples.input:
File names: Res.properties
Filter configuration ids: okf_properties
examples.output: All files named Res.properties (in any directory inside the ZIP file) will be extracted and processed using the Properties Filter with its default configuration.

examples.title: Multiple file types from an archive
examples.description: The default configuration extracts TMX and XLIFF files from the archive, using the appropriate filter for each format. File names and config IDs are comma-delimited and matched by position.
examples.input:
File names: *.tmx,*.xlf,*.xlff
Filter configuration ids: okf_tmx,okf_xliff,okf_xliff
examples.output: All .tmx files are processed with the TMX filter, and all .xlf/.xlff files are processed with the XLIFF filter.

okf_doxygen.overview: The Doxygen Filter extracts Doxygen-style comments from source code files for localization. It supports C++-style (///), Javadoc-style (/**), Qt-style (/*!), and Python-style (''' or """) comment blocks. The filter recognizes the full set of Doxygen special commands, HTML commands, and XML commands, interpreting them as inline codes or structural elements. It preserves line numbers so that a one-to-one correspondence between source and translated line numbers is maintained.

parser.description: Parser configuration group containing settings for Doxygen command definitions, HTML command definitions, and whitespace handling.

parser.preserveWhitespace.description: When set to true, prevents the filter from collapsing whitespace in extracted text. By default (false), the filter collapses single linebreaks within text runs, essentially merging each translatable paragraph into a single (potentially very long) line. Setting this to true preserves whitespace as-is in the extracted content.

parser.doxygenCommands.description: Defines how each Doxygen special command is handled during extraction. Each entry is keyed by the command name (without prefix, case-sensitive) and specifies the command type (PLACEHOLDER, OPENING, or CLOSING), whether it is inline, its paired command (for OPENING/CLOSING types), whether its content is translatable, and an ordered list of parameters. Parameters define their name, length (WORD, LINE, PHRASE, or PARAGRAPH), whether they are required, and whether they are translatable. WORD matches a single whitespace-delimited token, LINE matches to end of line, PHRASE matches a double-quoted string like '"image caption"', and PARAGRAPH matches to the next blank line or command. Untranslatable parameters following translatable ones are recorded as separate inline codes. Parameters should be listed in the order they appear after the command. Parameters with non-whitespace delimiters (e.g. '.py' in \code{.py}) are not currently supported.
notes: The parameters listing is optional for each command entry.
When present, parameters should be listed in the order in which they are written following the command.
Parameters with non-whitespace delimiters (e.g. '.py' in \code{.py}) are not currently supported.

parser.htmlCommands.description: Defines how HTML and XML commands within Doxygen comments are handled. Each entry is keyed by the tag name and specifies the command type (OPENING, CLOSING, or PLACEHOLDER) and whether it is an inline element. Supports standard HTML tags recognized by Doxygen (a, b, code, em, strong, table, etc.) as well as XML documentation tags (summary, param, returns, see, etc.).

inlineCodes.description: Configuration for pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.) within translatable text segments extracted from Doxygen comments.

inlineCodes.enabled.description: When enabled, applies regex-based pattern matching to detect inline codes within translatable text. These detected patterns are converted to inline code placeholders in the extracted segments.

inlineCodes.rules.description: Regex patterns used to identify inline codes in translatable text. Each rule specifies a pattern that, when matched, causes the matched text to be treated as a non-translatable inline code placeholder.

inlineCodes.mergeAdjacent.description: When enabled, consecutive inline codes that appear next to each other are merged into a single placeholder, reducing the number of inline code tags in the extracted segment.

inlineCodes.moveBoundaryCodes.description: When enabled, inline codes that appear at the beginning or end of a segment are moved to the skeleton (non-translatable portion), simplifying the translatable text.

inlineCodes.simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying the representation of inline codes in extracted segments.

path.description: Path to an external YAML configuration file containing the filter parameters.

limitations: Single linebreaks in a text run that are not part of a Doxygen command are collapsed. No effort is made to enforce a maximum line width upon output, so each translatable paragraph is collapsed to a single (potentially very long) line.
Command parameters with non-whitespace delimiters (e.g. '.py' in \code{.py}) are not currently supported.
Non-translatable command parameters are not exposed for any special processing.
processingNotes: If the file has a Unicode Byte-Order-Mark, the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used. Otherwise, the default encoding specified when opening the document is used.
The full set of Doxygen special commands, HTML commands, and XML commands are recognized and interpreted as inline codes or structural elements.
Line numbers are preserved so that a one-to-one correspondence between source line number and translated line number is maintained.
C++-style (///), Javadoc-style (/**), Qt-style (/*!), and Python-style (''' or """) comment blocks are all supported.

examples.title: Basic Doxygen comment extraction
examples.description: Shows how a Doxygen comment block with \class and \brief commands is extracted into text units with inline codes
examples.input:
/*! \class Test class.h "inc/class.h"
 *  \brief This is a test class.
 *
 * Some details about the Test class
 */
examples.output:
Text Unit 1: <1/><2/> This is a test class.
Text Unit 2: Some details about the Test class

examples.title: Various comment styles
examples.description: Demonstrates the different Doxygen comment styles supported by the filter, including Javadoc (/**), Qt (/*!), member (//!<, /**<), and inline (/*!<) styles
examples.output: Highlighted text is extracted: 'A test class', 'An enum type.', 'The documentation block cannot be put after the enum!', 'enum value 1', 'enum value 2', 'a member function.', 'an integer value'

examples.title: Custom command definition
examples.description: Shows how to define a custom Doxygen command in the YAML configuration with type, inline flag, pair, translatability, and parameter definitions
examples.input:
doxygen_commands:
  mycommand:
    type: PLACEHOLDER
    inline: true
    parameters:
      - name: name
        length: WORD
        required: true
        translatable: false
      - name: title
        length: LINE
        required: false
        translatable: true
examples.output: Defines a custom inline placeholder command 'mycommand' with a non-translatable WORD parameter and an optional translatable LINE parameter

examples.title: Custom regex-based command
examples.description: Shows how to define a custom command using a regex pattern that matches arbitrary text patterns within comments
examples.output: Matches runs of text like '###ACCESS_CHECKS###someValue;' and treats them as non-translatable placeholder codes

examples.title: Preserve whitespace configuration
examples.description: Shows how to prevent the filter from collapsing whitespace by enabling the preserve_whitespace setting
examples.output: Whitespace in extracted Doxygen comments is preserved as-is instead of being collapsed

okf_dtd.overview: The DTD Filter processes DTD (Document Type Definition) documents, specifically targeting XML-DTDs that contain translatable text entity declarations. A primary use case is Mozilla DTD files that accompany XUL documents used to build user-interface components. The filter extracts entity declaration values as translatable text units and supports inline code detection via regular expressions.

useCodeFinder.description: When enabled, activates pattern-based detection of inline codes within extracted text. This allows regular expressions defined in the code finder rules to identify spans of text (such as variables or placeholders) that should be treated as inline codes rather than translatable content. For example, enabling this with an appropriate rule can protect variable references like 'VAR1' from modification during translation.
notes: Used in conjunction with codeFinderRules to define the patterns that identify inline codes. The rules use the #v1 format with count and rule entries.
Regular expressions in YAML notation require double-escaping of backslashes (e.g., \bVAR\d\b becomes \\bVAR\\d\\b in YAML).

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.description: When enabled, inline codes that appear at the beginning or end of a segment are moved to the skeleton (non-translatable framework) rather than being included as part of the translatable content. This can simplify segments for translators by removing codes that bookend the text.

mergeAdjacentCodes.description: When enabled, consecutive inline codes that appear next to each other are merged into a single placeholder. This reduces the number of individual code placeholders translators need to manage when multiple codes appear in sequence.

simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying the representation of inline codes in extracted text. These rules define how complex inline code patterns can be reduced to simpler placeholder representations for easier handling during translation.

processingNotes: Input encoding is determined by BOM detection first; if no BOM is present, the default encoding specified in the filter options is used.
For UTF-8 output: a BOM is included only if the input was also UTF-8 and had a BOM. If converting from a non-UTF-8 encoding to UTF-8, no BOM is written.
Line-break style in the output matches the original input document's line-break type.

examples.title: DTD with translatable entity declarations
examples.description: Shows a typical DTD file with text entity declarations where the entity values are extracted as translatable text.
examples.output: The entity values 'Find Files', 'File', and 'Edit' are each extracted as individual translatable text units.

examples.title: Inline code detection with useCodeFinder
examples.description: Demonstrates using useCodeFinder and codeFinderRules to protect variable references (e.g., VAR1) as inline codes. The regular expression \bVAR\d\b matches variable patterns, but backslashes must be double-escaped in YAML notation.
examples.input:
useCodeFinder: true
codeFinderRules: "#v1\ncount.i=1\nrule0=\\bVAR\\d\\b"

<!ENTITY dialog.fileCount "Number of files = VAR1">
examples.output: The text 'VAR1' within the entity value is marked as an inline code placeholder, protecting it from modification during translation.

okf_epub.overview: The EPUB filter allows you to process EPUB (Electronic Publication) documents. It handles encoding detection via Byte-Order-Mark and supports configurable default encoding for input files.
processingNotes: If the file has a Unicode Byte-Order-Mark, the corresponding encoding (e.g. UTF-8, UTF-16) is used; otherwise the default encoding specified in filter options is used.
For UTF-8 output: if input was also UTF-8, a BOM is written only if one was detected in the input; if input was not UTF-8, no BOM is written.
No parameter editor is available; custom configurations must be edited with a text editor.
limitations: None known.

okf_html.overview: The HTML Filter is an Okapi component that implements the IFilter interface for HTML and XHTML documents. It supports both well-formed XHTML and non-well-formed HTML through configurable parsing modes. The filter uses YAML-based configuration files to define element and attribute extraction rules, controlling which content is translatable, localizable, or excluded. It ships with two built-in configurations: a minimalist default (okf_html) without structural groupings, and a maximalist well-formed variant (okf_html-wellFormed) that supports structural groupings like tables and lists.

parser.description: Parser behavior settings controlling how the HTML input is parsed.

parser.assumeWellformed.description: When set to true, the parser assumes the input HTML is well-formed XML, which enables faster parsing but may fail on non-conforming HTML. When false (default), the parser uses a more lenient HTML parsing mode that tolerates common HTML syntax issues. The well-formed mode is required when using structural GROUP rules (e.g., for table, ul, ol elements) — any structural tags mapped to groups must have both start and end tags, otherwise the filter will return an error.
notes: The predefined okf_html-wellFormed configuration sets this to true and includes GROUP rules for structural elements like table, ul, ol, dl, etc.
If structural groupings are needed, use the well-formed configuration, but all structural tags mapped to groups must be properly closed.

parser.preserveWhitespace.description: When true, preserves original whitespace in all extracted text globally. When false (default), whitespace is normalized except inside elements that have the PRESERVE_WHITESPACE rule type (e.g., <pre>). This is a global setting — for per-element whitespace preservation, use the PRESERVE_WHITESPACE rule type on specific elements instead.

skipEncodingDeclaration.description: Controls whether the filter skips processing of encoding declarations in the HTML document. When false (default), encoding declarations are processed normally.

attributes.description: Global attribute extraction rules that apply across multiple elements. Each key is a lowercase attribute name, and the value defines how that attribute should be handled across the document. Rules can be scoped using 'allElementsExcept' to exclude specific elements or 'onlyTheseElements' to restrict to specific elements. Attribute names must be lowercase in the configuration regardless of their casing in the document. Attributes with a prefix (e.g., xml:lang) should be declared with the prefix enclosed in single quotes.
notes: All attribute names must be in lowercase in the configuration file, regardless of their casing in the document.
Prefixed attributes (e.g., xml:lang) must be enclosed in single quotes in the YAML configuration.

elements.description: Element extraction rules that map HTML element names to their processing behavior. Each key is a lowercase element name, and the value defines how the filter treats that element and its attributes. Elements can be classified as INLINE (flows within text), TEXTUNIT (extracted as translatable segments), GROUP (structural containers), EXCLUDE (skipped), INCLUDE (exception to exclusion), ATTRIBUTES_ONLY (only attributes extracted), or PRESERVE_WHITESPACE (whitespace kept as-is). Element names must be lowercase. Regex patterns can be used as element names for conditional rules (e.g., '.*' with conditions to match elements with translate='no').
notes: All element names must be in lowercase in the configuration file.
Elements with GROUP rule type must be well-formed (have both start and end tags) when assumeWellformed is true, otherwise the filter returns an error.
The '.*' and '.+' regex patterns with conditions enable support for the HTML5 translate attribute.
TEXTUNIT elements carry their surrounding tags along with any extracted text.
ATTRIBUTES_ONLY with translatableAttributes implies the tag will become a TEXTUNIT with embedded skeleton.

inlineCodes.description: Configuration for pattern-based detection of inline codes within extracted text. Allows defining regular expressions to identify placeholders, variables, or other non-translatable spans that should be protected as inline codes.

inlineCodes.enabled.description: Enable pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, tags, variables, etc.) within extracted text. When true, the patterns defined in the rules are applied to identify spans that should be treated as inline codes rather than translatable text. Corresponds to the 'useCodeFinder' parameter in the YAML configuration.

inlineCodes.rules.description: Regular expression patterns used to detect inline codes within extracted text. Each rule defines a regex pattern that matches text spans to be treated as non-translatable inline codes. For example, a pattern like '\bVAR\d\b' would match 'VAR1', 'VAR2', etc. In YAML notation, backslashes in regex patterns must be double-escaped. Corresponds to the 'codeFinderRules' parameter in the YAML configuration.
notes: In YAML notation, backslashes in regular expressions must be escaped (e.g., '\\bVAR\\d\\b' for the regex '\bVAR\d\b').
An alternate YAML block scalar syntax using '|-' can be used for easier readability of multi-line rules.

inlineCodes.mergeAdjacent.description: When true, consecutive inline codes that appear next to each other are merged into a single placeholder. This can simplify the translatable segments by reducing the number of individual code placeholders.

inlineCodes.moveBoundaryCodes.description: When true, inline codes that appear at the beginning or end of a segment are moved to the skeleton (non-translatable portion). This cleans up segments by removing leading/trailing codes that translators don't need to see or reposition.

inlineCodes.simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation in extracted segments. These rules control how inline codes are presented to translators.

limitations: Content of <style> and <script> elements is not extracted.
Tags from server-side scripts such as PHP, ASPX, JSP, etc. are not formally supported and will be treated as non-translatable.
processingNotes: Encoding detection: If the document has an encoding declaration it is used; otherwise the default encoding specified in filter options is used.
UTF-8 BOM handling: If both input and output are UTF-8, a BOM is written only if one was detected in the input. If the input was not UTF-8 but output is UTF-8, no BOM is written.
If the input file has no declared encoding, the filter tries to add a <meta> tag (HTML) or <meta /> tag (XHTML) in output, but only if there is a <head> element in the file.
Line-break preservation: The type of line-breaks in the output matches the original input.
Character and numeric entities are converted to Unicode. Entities defined in a DTD or schema are passed through without change.

examples.title: Inline code finder for variables
examples.description: Using the code finder to protect variable placeholders like VAR1 from modification during translation
examples.output:
In

Number of files = VAR1

, 'VAR1' is treated as an inline code placeholder

examples.title: Inline code finder alternate YAML syntax
examples.description: Using YAML block scalar syntax for more readable code finder rules
examples.output: Same result as escaped syntax — VAR1 is detected as inline code

examples.title: Simple translatable attributes
examples.description: Specifying attributes that should always be translatable on an element, using a simple list
examples.output: The accesskey, area, and alt attributes of <area> elements are extracted as translatable

examples.title: Conditional translatable attributes
examples.description: Specifying translatable attributes with conditions — the input element's alt, value, accesskey, and title are translatable only when the type attribute is not file, hidden, image, or password
examples.input:
input:
  ruleTypes: [INLINE]
  translatableAttributes:
    alt: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
    value: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
    accesskey: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
    title: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
examples.output: For <input type="text">, alt/value/accesskey/title are translatable. For <input type="hidden">, they are not.

examples.title: Character entity references in output
examples.description: Using escapeCharacters to control which extended characters are output as HTML character entity references
examples.output:
© €µÆĄ

— only Ą (U+0104) is not represented as an entity because no HTML entity is defined for it

examples.title: Exclude by default configuration
examples.description: Setting exclude_by_default to true so only explicitly configured elements are extracted — here only <title> content is translatable
examples.output: Only the content of <title> elements is extracted for translation; all other elements are excluded.

examples.title: Inline CDATA handling
examples.description: Treating CDATA sections as inline elements to avoid breaking text flow
examples.output:
Text with CDATA

is extracted with CDATA markers treated as inline opening/closing tags

examples.title: Quote mode configuration
examples.description: Configuring how quote and apostrophe characters are escaped in output. Available modes: 0=unescaped, 1=all escaped, 2=double to named + single to numeric, 3=double quotes only.
examples.output: Only double quote characters are escaped to named entities in the output; single quotes are left unescaped.

okf_html5.overview: This filter processes HTML5 documents that may contain ITS 2.0 (Internationalization Tag Set) markup. Input documents are expected to be valid HTML5. The filter supports global and local ITS rules and most ITS 2.0 data categories, with default behavior based on ITS defaults for language information, id values, within-text elements, and translate attributes. Default behavior can be overridden via ITS markup in the document or by specifying an ITS parameters file.

simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation in the extracted content. These rules control how inline codes (tags) are represented in a simplified form during processing.

path.description: Path to an ITS rules file that overrides the default ITS behavior. The parameters file used by the filter is an ITS document. When specified, the rules in this file override the default ITS processing behavior (lang attribute for Language Information, id attribute for Id Value, phrasing content elements as withinText, and translate attribute handling).

limitations: This filter is BETA.
processingNotes: The filter decides which input encoding to use via the detection mechanism defined by the HTML5 specification.
Output encoding matches input encoding unless overridden by the calling tool.
Line-break types in the output match those of the original input.

Escaping of quote and apostrophe characters can be configured via quoteMode: 0 = no escaping, 1 = escape both to named entities, 2 = double quotes to named entity and single quotes to numeric entity, 3 = escape double quotes only.
Most phrasing content elements are interpreted as withinText="yes" by default.
The translate attribute behavior differs from standard XML — it follows the HTML5 specification definition.
The lang attribute is used by default for the Language Information data category.
The id attribute is used by default for the Id Value data category.

okf_icml.overview: The ICML Filter processes WCML/ICML (InCopy Markup Language) documents, which are XML-based formats used by Adobe InDesign/InCopy. It is very similar to the IDML Filter. The filter supports extraction of notes, master spreads, and provides options for inline code simplification and handling of large spread files.

extraction.extractNotes.description: When enabled, extracts the content of notes (<Note> elements) from the ICML document as translator notes.

extraction.extractMasterSpreads.description: When enabled, extracts the content of master spreads if they exist in the document. If this option is not set, only the normal spreads are extracted.

extraction.simplifyCodes.description: When enabled, reduces the number of inline codes by re-grouping adjacent codes when it is possible. This simplifies the translatable segments by merging consecutive inline formatting codes.

extraction.skipThreshold.description: Sets the maximum size for the spread files (in KBytes). Any spread file above the given value will either generate an error or will be skipped from extraction depending on the specified option. This allows you to skip over large spread files that may contain only graphics and require too much memory to be opened. Valid range is 1 to 32000.
notes: Note: skipped files are not checked for translatable text.

extraction.newTuOnBr.description:
Controls whether a Br (break) tag creates a new text unit (segment). When enabled,
elements in the ICML content will cause the filter to start a new paragraph/text unit at that point. Default is false.

limitations: The wiki states limitations are TODO — no specific limitations are documented.
processingNotes: Processing details are not yet documented in the wiki (marked as TODO).

okf_idml.overview: The IDML filter processes IDML (InDesign Markup Language) documents, an XML-based format introduced in Adobe InDesign CS4 for representing InDesign content. IDML is used in several InDesign and InCopy file types. The filter extracts translatable text from spreads, stories, notes, master spreads, hidden layers, and other IDML elements, with configurable options for handling inline codes, formatting tolerances, and content exclusion.

inlineCodes.description: Inline code detection and handling settings. When enabled, uses regex patterns to identify inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.) within translatable text. Supports merging adjacent codes, moving boundary codes to skeleton, and simplifying inline code representation.

extraction.description: Group of settings controlling what content is extracted from the IDML document, including notes, master spreads, hidden layers, hyperlinks, index topics, and other structural elements.

extraction.extractNotes.description: When enabled, extracts the content of notes (<Note> elements) in the IDML document as translator notes. By default, notes are not extracted.

extraction.extractMasterSpreads.description: When enabled, extracts the content of the master spreads if they exist. If this option is not set, only the normal spreads are extracted. Enabled by default.

extraction.extractCustomTextVariables.description: When enabled, extracts custom text variables for translation. Disabled by default.

extraction.extractExternalHyperlinks.description: When enabled, the external hyperlinks are extracted for translation. Disabled by default.

extraction.extractHiddenLayers.description: When enabled, extracts content from hidden layers in addition to visible layers. By default, hidden layers are not extracted.

extraction.extractHiddenPasteboardItems.description: When enabled, extracts content from hidden pasteboard items. Disabled by default.

extraction.extractHyperlinkTextSourcesIn.description: When set to true, the hyperlink text sources are extracted inline. When set to false (default), they are represented as referencing groups of textual units.

extraction.extractIndexTopics.description: When enabled, extracts index topics for translation. Disabled by default.

extraction.extractBreaksInline.description: When enabled, break elements are extracted as inline codes rather than causing text unit boundaries. Disabled by default.

extraction.untagXmlStructures.description: When enabled, skips embedded XML structural information when extracting translatable content. This removes XML structure tags that InDesign may embed in the IDML content. Enabled by default.

extraction.skipDiscretionaryHyphens.description: When enabled, discretionary hyphens (soft hyphens inserted by InDesign for line-break hints) are skipped during extraction. Disabled by default.

extraction.specialCharacterPattern.description: A pattern of special characters where matched content is treated as inline code. The default pattern includes various Unicode special characters used by InDesign (non-breaking spaces, zero-width joiners/non-joiners, soft hyphens, non-breaking hyphens, etc.).

extraction.maxAttributeSize.description: Sets the size in bytes for the attribute buffer. The default is 4MB (4 * 1024 * 1024 = 4194304). This controls the maximum size of XML attributes the filter can process.

formatting.description: Group of settings controlling formatting tolerance thresholds. These options allow the filter to ignore small differences in character formatting properties (kerning, tracking, leading, baseline shift) within specified min/max ranges, reducing unnecessary inline code segmentation caused by minor formatting variations.

formatting.ignoreCharacterKerning.description: When enabled, ignores kerning differences between characters when determining whether to create separate inline codes for differently-formatted text runs. Disabled by default.

dependsOn.condition: should be set to define the minimum kerning threshold when ignoring is enabled
should be set to define the maximum kerning threshold when ignoring is enabled

formatting.kerningMin.description: The minimum kerning value threshold below which kerning differences are ignored. Used in conjunction with ignoreCharacterKerning to define the tolerance range.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

formatting.kerningMax.description: The maximum kerning value threshold above which kerning differences are no longer ignored. Used in conjunction with ignoreCharacterKerning to define the tolerance range.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

formatting.ignoreCharacterTracking.description: When enabled, ignores tracking differences between characters when determining whether to create separate inline codes. Disabled by default.

dependsOn.condition: should be set to define the minimum tracking threshold when ignoring is enabled
should be set to define the maximum tracking threshold when ignoring is enabled

formatting.trackingMin.description: The minimum tracking value threshold below which tracking differences are ignored.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

formatting.trackingMax.description: The maximum tracking value threshold above which tracking differences are no longer ignored.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

formatting.ignoreCharacterLeading.description: When enabled, ignores leading (line spacing) differences between characters when determining whether to create separate inline codes. Disabled by default.

dependsOn.condition: should be set to define the minimum leading threshold when ignoring is enabled
should be set to define the maximum leading threshold when ignoring is enabled

formatting.leadingMin.description: The minimum leading value threshold below which leading differences are ignored.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

formatting.leadingMax.description: The maximum leading value threshold above which leading differences are no longer ignored.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

formatting.ignoreCharacterBaselineShift.description: When enabled, ignores baseline shift differences between characters when determining whether to create separate inline codes. Disabled by default.

dependsOn.condition: should be set to define the minimum baseline shift threshold when ignoring is enabled
should be set to define the maximum baseline shift threshold when ignoring is enabled

formatting.baselineShiftMin.description: The minimum baseline shift value threshold below which baseline shift differences are ignored.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

formatting.baselineShiftMax.description: The maximum baseline shift value threshold above which baseline shift differences are no longer ignored.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

extractMathZones.description: When enabled (default is true), math zones in the IDML document are extracted for translation.

excludedStyleConfigurations.description: When enabled, content with the specified styles is excluded from extraction. This allows filtering out content based on InDesign paragraph or character styles.

processingNotes: The filter processes all spreads in the document, and for each spread gathers the list of stories used in <TextFrame> and <TextPath> elements.
Text is extracted by spread, and within each spread by story in the order they appear in the spread.
Stories embedded inside other stories and not declared at a spread level are extracted in a special group.

The default special character pattern includes various Unicode characters used by InDesign (non-breaking spaces, zero-width joiners/non-joiners, soft hyphens, non-breaking hyphens, BOM characters) which are treated as inline codes.

okf_json.overview: The JSON Filter is an Okapi component that implements the IFilter interface for JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) files, based on the JSON specifications at json.org. It extracts translatable string values from JSON key-value pairs and supports configurable extraction rules, inline code detection, sub-filtering, metadata extraction, and note/ID assignment via regex-based key matching.

inlineCodes.description: Inline code detection and handling. When enabled, specified regular expressions are applied to extracted text and any matches are converted to inline codes. Cannot be used together with the sub-filtering option. The default expression matches printf-style format specifiers, common escape sequences (\r\n, \a, \b, \f, \n, \r, \t, \v), and curly-brace placeholders like {0}.
notes: Note: This option cannot be used together with the sub-filtering option.

extraction.description: Control what content is extracted for translation. Provides options to extract all key-value pairs or use regex-based extraction rules, include isolated strings from arrays, exclude specific keys, and apply sub-filters to embedded content.

extraction.extractAll.description: When true, extracts all strings that have an associated key. If exceptions are defined, strings whose keys match the exception expression are not extracted. When false (Do not extract key/string pairs), no keyed strings are extracted unless they match the exception expression. The exception regex inverts the default behavior: in extract-all mode it excludes matches, in do-not-extract mode it includes matches.
notes: If extraction rules (pathRules) are specified (>= M39), they override this setting and the exception-based rules.

extraction.pathRules.description: Regex matching keys whose values are extracted. If specified, this overrides the extractAll setting and the exception-based extraction rules. Applied to key names (or full key paths if useFullPath is enabled). For example: /widgets/body.* to extract only body content under widgets.
notes: If specified, these will override the corresponding extractAll/exception rules above.

extraction.includeIsolatedStrings.description: Extract string values that are not associated directly to a key, such as strings in arrays. When enabled, standalone string values in JSON arrays are extracted as translatable content.

extraction.excludeKeys.description: A comma-separated list of keys (or a regular expression) that should be excluded from or included in extraction, depending on the extractAll setting. When extractAll is true, keys matching this expression are excluded. When extractAll is false, keys matching this expression are the only ones extracted. In combination with useFullPath, you can exclude all nested elements in a JSON structure with patterns like ^.*?/excludedStructure/.*

extraction.subfilterRules.description: Specify an Okapi filter ID (e.g. okf_html) to process the content of all translatable text with that sub-filter. Leave blank for default behavior. The sub-filter processes the extracted string values through the specified filter, enabling handling of embedded markup or other structured content within JSON values.
notes: Note: This option cannot be used together with the inline codes (useCodeFinder) option.

keys.description: Configure how JSON keys map to text unit identification. Controls whether keys are used as resource names, whether the full hierarchical path is used, and rules for ID assignment.

keys.useAsName.description: Use the value of the JSON key as the name (resname) of the extracted text unit. When enabled, the immediate key name is used as the resource name in output formats like XLIFF.

keys.useFullPath.description: Use the full hierarchical key path (e.g. /menu/value/popup/menuitem/value) as the resource name instead of just the immediate key. The useAsName option must be enabled for this to take effect. When enabled, exception regular expressions also apply to the full path rather than just the key name.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

keys.leadingSlash.description: Include a leading '/' character in the full key path. Only relevant when useFullPath is enabled. Controls whether paths appear as '/menu/value' (with slash) or 'menu/value' (without).

dependsOn.condition: must be true

keys.useIdStack.description: Build TextUnit IDs from the nested key stack. When enabled, IDs are constructed by accumulating keys through the JSON hierarchy.

keys.idRules.description: Regex matching keys whose values become the text unit ID (resname in XLIFF). This overrides the useAsName behavior. Useful when the JSON structure contains explicit ID fields alongside translatable text — for example, when an array contains objects with both 'key' and 'text' fields, and you want the value of 'key' to become the resname of the text unit containing 'text'.
notes: If specified, overrides the useAsName setting.

metadata.description: Configure metadata extraction rules for notes, generic metadata, and max-width constraints.

metadata.noteRules.description: Regex matching keys whose values become translator notes. The values of matching keys are transferred to <note> elements in XLIFF output. Applied to key names (or full key paths if configured).

metadata.metaRules.description: Regex matching keys whose values are added as generic metadata to the text unit. In XLIFF output, these appear as <context-group> elements.

metadata.maxwidthRules.description: Regex matching keys whose numeric values are extracted as maxwidth properties in XLIFF. The extracted value is used as the maxwidth constraint for all other elements of the array at that level. Rules: (1) Only one matching array element per hierarchy level is allowed. (2) For nested array levels, different maxwidth values can be defined for parent-child and sibling levels. (3) Different definitions can share the same key name. (4) If no key matches at a sublevel but one matches at a higher level, the higher-level definition determines the max length for the deepest hierarchy level and its siblings without a matching key. (5) If keys match at both higher and lower levels, values are extracted for all corresponding levels, but the higher-level matching key must appear after the last child element.

output.description: Output formatting options for the generated JSON.

output.escapeSlashes.description: Escape forward slashes in output JSON as \/. When enabled, forward slash characters in string values are escaped in the output. This matches the behavior of some JSON serializers that escape slashes for safe embedding in HTML <script> tags.

maxwidthSizeUnit.description: The string value to use as the size-unit attribute of the trans-unit in XLIFF when maxwidth properties are extracted via maxwidthRules. For example, 'char' or 'pixel'.

dependsOn.condition: must be set to extract maxwidth properties

limitations: Comments within a JSON string are parsed as part of the string content, not as comments. A configured subfilter will then process these as true comments (they will become part of the skeleton or whatever the filter is configured to do).
processingNotes: JSON files are normally in Unicode encoding, but the filter supports any encoding. If a Unicode BOM is present, the corresponding encoding is used. Otherwise, if a charset declaration exists in the first 1000 characters, that encoding is used (with BOM taking precedence if both exist and conflict). Otherwise, the default encoding is used.
For UTF-8 output: a BOM is written only if the input was UTF-8 and had a BOM. If converting from another encoding to UTF-8, no BOM is written.
The type of line-breaks in the output matches the original input.
Though not technically legal in JSON, the filter supports four comment types: // comment, # comment, /* comment */, and <!-- comment -->.

examples.title: Basic JSON extraction
examples.description: A simple JSON file showing which string values are extracted as translatable content (highlighted with span tags in wiki).
examples.output: Extracted text units: "File", "New", "Open", "Close"

examples.title: Using idRules to extract resname from sibling key
examples.description: When JSON contains arrays of objects with a 'key' field alongside a 'text' field, using idRules with regex 'key' causes the value of the 'key' field to become the resname of the text unit.
examples.output:
<trans-unit id="tu1" resname="datePicker_marchMonth" xml:space="preserve">
  <source xml:lang="en-US">March</source>
</trans-unit>
<trans-unit id="tu2" resname="datePicker_aprilMonth" xml:space="preserve">
  <source xml:lang="en-US">April</source>
</trans-unit>

examples.title: FPRM extraction rules example
examples.description: Example regex rules applied to key names for extraction, notes, IDs, and metadata. Shows how different rule types target different key paths under /widgets/.
examples.output: Keys matching /widgets/body.* are extracted; /widgets/name.* values become notes; /widgets/id.* values become resnames; /widgets/image.* values become metadata.

examples.title: Excluding nested structures with full key path
examples.description: Using excludeKeys with useFullPath enabled to exclude all nested elements under a specific JSON structure.
examples.output: All key-value pairs under any 'excludedStructure' node at any depth are excluded from extraction.

okf_markdown.overview: The Markdown Filter extracts translatable text from Markdown files. It is designed to work with markdown based on the CommonMark specification, with additional features to support GitHub-flavored Markdown. The filter handles HTML inline elements and blocks via a configurable HTML sub-filter, and supports YAML front matter extraction, code block handling, and URL translation.

inlineCodes.description: Inline code detection and handling settings. The Inline Code Finder is supported by this filter and applies to translatable text within the proper Markdown document. It does not apply to HTML inline tags or HTML blocks — for those, you need to enable inline code patterns on the HTML filter separately.
notes: The Inline Code Finder support was temporarily unavailable in some snapshot builds of version 0.36, but has been restored.

For HTML inline tags or HTML blocks, you must configure the inline code pattern on the HTML filter separately, name the configuration as okf_html@arbitrary-name.fprm, and specify that name for the htmlSubfilter parameter.

extraction.description: Controls which content types within the Markdown document are extracted for translation, including URLs, code blocks, image alt text, YAML front matter, and MDX syntax.

extraction.translateImageAltText.description: Controls whether image alt text is extracted for translation. This applies to both Markdown image syntax (![alt text](url)) and HTML img tags (). When true, the alt text will be extracted as translatable content.

extraction.translateUrls.description: Controls whether URLs in link and image statements are exposed for translation. By default, URLs are not extracted. When enabled, matching URLs will be extracted as a subflow — meaning the URL text unit appears before the segment that contains it in the output.

extraction.urlPattern.description: A regular expression pattern that controls which URLs are extracted for translation when translateUrls is enabled. Only URLs matching this pattern will be extracted. The default value '.+' matches all URLs.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

extraction.translateCodeBlocks.description: Controls whether the contents of fenced code blocks (delimited by triple backticks or tildes) are exposed for translation.

extraction.translateIndentedCodeBlocks.description: Controls whether the contents of indented code blocks (lines indented by 4 spaces or 1 tab) are exposed for translation.

extraction.translateInlineCodeBlocks.description: Controls whether the contents of inline code blocks (text delimited by single backticks) are exposed for translation.

extraction.translateHeaderMetadata.description: Controls whether the YAML metadata header is exposed for translation. Some markdown formats support a YAML Metadata Header (as defined by Pandoc's yaml_metadata_block extension) containing key/value data. When enabled, the header will be parsed and metadata values will be exposed for translation. A custom YAML sub-filter can be specified via the yamlSubfilter parameter to control which metadata fields are extracted.

extraction.nonTranslateBlocks.description: A comma-separated list of strings that prevents matching block quotes from being translated. Block quotes whose content starts with one of the specified strings will not be extracted. When empty (default), contents in all block quotes will be extracted.

extraction.parseMdx.description: Experimental. When enabled, parses out certain MDX expressions using regex. Specifically, multi-line 'export' blocks are treated as skeleton (non-translatable content).
notes: This feature is marked as experimental.

output.description: Controls output formatting options including header anchor generation, character escaping, and backslash escape handling.

output.generateHeaderAnchors.description: Controls whether anchors are automatically generated for headings in the source document. Some markdown parsers support explicit named anchors in header markup using the syntax {#my-anchor}. When enabled, this option generates stable anchors for the purpose of providing reliable hyperlink targets that reference translatable header values.

output.charactersToEscape.description: A string of punctuation characters that will be backslash-escaped on export when unescapeBackslashCharacters is enabled. Each character in the string is treated as an individual character to escape.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

output.htmlEntitiesToEscape.description: A string of characters that will be encoded as HTML entities on export. Each character in the string will be converted to its corresponding HTML entity representation. When empty (default), no additional HTML entity encoding is performed.

output.unescapeBackslashCharacters.description: Controls support for backslash escaping of punctuation characters. When enabled, the filter parses backslash-escaped punctuation in source documents and uses the charactersToEscape list to determine which characters to backslash-escape on export.

subfilters.description: Configuration for sub-filters used to process HTML content and YAML front matter within Markdown documents.

subfilters.htmlSubfilter.description: The custom configuration ID of the HTML filter that will be called to process HTML contents within Markdown documents. The configuration file must be saved in a known location with a '.fprm' suffix. When left empty (default), a default HTML filter configuration tailored for the Markdown filter is used. To configure inline code patterns for HTML content, create a custom HTML filter configuration named okf_html@arbitrary-name.fprm and specify that name here.

subfilters.yamlSubfilter.description: The custom configuration ID of the YAML filter that will be called to process any YAML metadata header detected in the document. This allows customization of which metadata fields are extracted for translation. When left empty (default), the default YAML filter configuration is used.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for the YAML sub-filter to be invoked

processingNotes: If the file has a Unicode Byte-Order-Mark (BOM), the corresponding encoding (e.g., UTF-8, UTF-16) is used. Otherwise, the default encoding specified in the filter options is used.
HTML inline elements (tags) and HTML blocks (text between block-forming start/end tags) are processed by the HTML filter, which can be customized separately via the htmlSubfilter parameter.

The Inline Code Finder applies only to translatable text within the Markdown portion of the document — not to HTML inline tags or HTML blocks. Those require separate inline code configuration on the HTML sub-filter.

examples.title: URL translation as subflows
examples.description: When translateUrls is enabled, URLs are extracted as subflows that appear before the segment containing them. This example shows a Markdown image link and its XLIFF extraction, where the URL is extracted as a separate translation unit (tu2) referenced from the main text unit (tu1).
examples.input: Please click ![The Information desk logo](images/circled-i.jpg) for help.
examples.output:
<trans-unit id="tu2" restype="x-img-link" xml:space="preserve">
<source xml:lang="en">images/circled-i.jpg</source>
</trans-unit>
<trans-unit id="tu1" xml:space="preserve">
<source xml:lang="en">Please click <bpt id="1">![</bpt>The Information desk logo<ept id="1">]([#$tu2])</ept> for help.</source>
</trans-unit>

okf_messageformat.overview: The MessageFormatFilter handles message formats commonly found in software applications, including ICU Message Format and Java MessageFormat. It is designed to be used as a subfilter, where message strings can reside in various container formats such as JSON, YAML, XML, etc.

addPluralForms.description: When enabled, new plural forms will be added based on the target locale. The source string is modified and then refiltered to provide the new plural forms to the translator. Default is false.

normalize.description: When enabled, the source will be normalized to move leading and trailing text inside each complex (plural, select, etc.) variant. This makes translation easier by forcing each variant to be a complete phrase or sentence. Default is false.
notes: This option can increase word and character counts for the source!

prettyPrint.description: When enabled, the output is formatted to enhance readability. Any whitespace added is not significant. The default option (false) returns a compact string, normally a single line. Default is false.

limitations: The 'choice' syntax is not supported. For example: 'The value {count, choice, 0 #is none |1 #is one |1 <is more than one}'. This syntax is deprecated and should be converted to 'select' or 'plural'.
processingNotes: This filter is designed to be used as a subfilter — it processes message strings extracted by a parent filter from container formats like JSON, YAML, or XML.

okf_mif.overview: The MIF Filter processes MIF (Maker Interchange Format) documents generated and read by Adobe FrameMaker. It supports extraction of body pages, master pages, reference pages, hidden pages, variables, index markers, and cross-reference links. The MIF specification for version 9.0 is available on the Adobe Web site.

extraction.extractVariables.description: When enabled, extracts the definitions of the variables defined in the MIF document.

extraction.extractIndexMarkers.description: When enabled, extracts the index markers in the extractable pages. The text of each index entry is extracted in a separate text unit, placed before the text unit that contains the index marker.

extraction.extractLinks.description: When enabled, extracts URLs of the links in the extractable pages. Each URL is extracted in a separate text unit, placed before the text unit that contains the hypertext marker.

extraction.extractBodyPages.description: When enabled, extracts the body pages of the MIF document.

extraction.extractHiddenPages.description: When enabled, extracts the hidden pages of the MIF document.

extraction.extractMasterPages.description: When enabled, extracts the master pages of the MIF document.

extraction.extractReferencePages.description: When enabled, extracts the reference pages of the MIF document. Note that by default FrameMaker creates its new documents with several reference pages that contain text.
notes: By default FrameMaker creates its new documents with several reference pages that contain text.

inlineCodes.enabled.description: When enabled, uses the specified regular expressions on the text of the extracted items. Any match will be converted to an inline code. By default the expression is: <\$.*?>

limitations: Versions < MIF 8.0 are not supported.
You may run into Java heap memory issues if the document includes very large embedded insets (e.g. images). The workaround is to link to external objects rather than embed them.

The filter does not do font mapping yet, so if the translated file is in a language not supported by the fonts used in the source document, you need to update the paragraph and character catalogs to use fonts providing the proper support.
processingNotes: The encoding of the input MIF document is automatically detected based on the version of the file and different other information in the document.
MIF v8 and above use UTF-8.
MIF v8 and above are automatically output in UTF-8.

examples.title: Default inline code pattern
examples.description: The default inline code regular expression pattern used by the MIF filter to detect FrameMaker variables in extracted text.

okf_mosestext.overview: The Moses Text Filter processes InlineText files used by the Moses MT system. Each line in the file is read as a separate text unit, with inline codes represented by XLIFF-like elements (`<g>`, `<x>`, `<bx>`, `<ex>`) and line-breaks represented by `<lb/>`. The filter is typically used in conjunction with the Moses InlineText Extraction Step and Moses InlineText Leveraging Step for round-trip processing of various document formats through Moses.
processingNotes: If the document has a BOM, it is used to determine the input encoding; otherwise UTF-8 is used as the default encoding regardless of any specified default.
The output encoding is always forced to UTF-8.
The type of line-breaks in the output matches the original input.

Reading a Moses InlineText file may yield more text units than the original document because each segment (or unsegmented text unit) of the original is extracted as a single entry. Multiple segments from one original text unit become separate entries with no grouping information in the Moses file.
limitations: None known.

examples.title: Moses InlineText file format
examples.description: Example of a Moses InlineText file with four text units. Translatable text appears directly on each line, while inline codes use XLIFF-like markup elements (<g>, <x>, <bx>, <ex>). Line-breaks within an entry are represented by <lb/>.
examples.input:
Text in the first entry.
Text of the second entry<lb/>which spans<lb/>several lines
Third entry.
Fourth entry with <g id="1">bold words</g> and some code:<x id="2"/>
examples.output: Four separate text units are extracted, one per line.

okf_multiparsers.overview: The Multi-Parsers Filter is an Okapi component for extracting translatable text from two-level complex formats. For example, it can process a CSV file where some columns contain Markdown, some HTML, and some plain text. By default, the filter settings are configured to process a CSV file where all columns are translatable and treated as plain text.

csvAutoDetectColumnTypes.description: When enabled, the filter uses auto-detection to determine column types from a special row in each input file rather than relying on manual column type configuration. When this is enabled, the manual column configuration options (csvFormatCols, csvNoExtractCols, csvStartingRow) are disabled and the filter reads type information from the row specified by csvAutoDetectColumnTypesRow.
notes: The wiki parameters section is marked as TODO, so detailed behavioral documentation is not yet available.

csvAutoDetectColumnTypesRow.description: Specifies the row number (1-based) at which the special configuration/type-detection information is located in the input file. This row contains metadata that tells the filter how to treat each column. Valid range: 1 to 9999999.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

csvFormatCols.description: A comma-separated list of columns with non-plain-text content. Each entry specifies a column index (0-based) followed by a colon and the filter configuration ID to use for that column's content. For example, '3:okf_markdown' means column 3 should be parsed as Markdown. Columns not listed here are treated as plain text by default.

dependsOn.condition: must be false (disabled when auto-detection is enabled)

csvNoExtractCols.description: A comma-separated list of column indices (0-based) that should not be extracted for translation. Columns listed here will be skipped during extraction.

dependsOn.condition: must be false (disabled when auto-detection is enabled)

csvStartingRow.description: The row number (1-based) at which extraction starts. Rows before this number are skipped. Valid range: 1 to 9999999. Default is 1 (start from the first row).

dependsOn.condition: must be false (disabled when auto-detection is enabled)

limitations: This filter is BETA.
processingNotes: If the input file has a Unicode Byte-Order-Mark (BOM), the corresponding encoding (e.g. UTF-8, UTF-16) is used automatically.
If no BOM is present, the default encoding specified in the filter options is used.

examples.title: CSV with mixed content types
examples.description: Configure the filter to process a CSV file where column 3 contains Markdown content while other columns are plain text. Use csvFormatCols to specify the Markdown column.

okf_odf.overview: The OpenOffice Filter is an Okapi component that implements the IFilter interface for OpenOffice.org documents: ODT (text), ODS (spreadsheet), ODP (slides), ODG (graphics), and their corresponding template formats. These documents use the OpenDocument format (ODF). If you need to process directly an XML ODF file, you can use the ODF Filter that the OpenOffice Filter uses internally.

extraction.extractNotes.description: Set this option to extract the content of <office:annotation> elements (notes) as translatable text. If this option is not set, notes are not extracted.
notes: The option to extract or not the notes is not working yet — notes will be extracted regardless of the option setting.

extraction.extractReferences.description: Set this option to extract the content of <text:bookmark-ref> elements. The content of these elements is only a copy of the content of the referent. It is updated automatically within OpenOffice, so any translation done for this content will be automatically overwritten as soon as the document is updated. However, in some cases it may be useful to have the referenced text as part of the segment where it is inserted.
notes: The option to extract or not the references is not working yet — they will be extracted regardless of the option setting.

extraction.extractMetadata.description: Extract metadata fields in meta.xml for translation. When enabled, the meta.xml sub-document will contain translatable text units for document metadata such as title, subject, and keywords.

extraction.encodeCharacterEntityReferenc.description: Encode character entity reference glyphs. When enabled (default), character entity reference glyphs are encoded in the output.

inlineCodes.enabled.description: Enable pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.) within translatable text. When enabled, the rules defined in the code finder rules are applied to identify inline codes.

inlineCodes.rules.description: Regex patterns used to identify inline codes in translatable text. Each rule defines a regular expression pattern that matches content to be treated as an inline code (placeholder) rather than translatable text.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

inlineCodes.mergeAdjacent.description: Merge consecutive inline codes into a single placeholder. This can simplify the appearance of segments that contain multiple adjacent inline codes.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

inlineCodes.moveBoundaryCodes.description: Move inline codes that appear at the beginning or end of a segment to the skeleton (non-translatable portion). This simplifies segments by removing leading and trailing codes that translators do not need to see.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

inlineCodes.simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation. These rules control how inline codes are displayed to translators, making complex code sequences easier to work with.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

limitations: Some deleted text may get extracted. Make sure you have accepted or rejected the revision changes before processing the input document, as currently some text marked as deleted is still extracted.
The options to extract or not the notes and the references are not working yet. They will be extracted regardless of the option settings.
Sequential tabs may get reduced to a single tab during an extraction and merge round trip: the elements for spaces and tabs are supported in output but still incorrectly handled on input.
The target (output) encoding must be set to UTF-8 when extracting the documents to merge them back properly.
processingNotes: Input encoding is automatically detected.
Any user-specified output encoding is ignored by these filters — they always use UTF-8.
Line-breaks in the output are always set to a simple linefeed (LF).
An OpenOffice document is a ZIP file with several sub-documents inside. The main one (content.xml) contains the body of the data, but meta.xml and style.xml may also contain translatable text.

All embedded files are treated as sub-documents by the filter. For example, a single ODT extracted to XLIFF produces three <file> elements: one for content.xml, one for style.xml, and one for meta.xml. Very often only content.xml has extracted text.

okf_openxml.overview: The OpenXML filter processes Microsoft Office documents from 2007 and later, including DOCX (text documents), XLSX (spreadsheets), and PPTX (presentations). These documents use the OpenXML format, as opposed to the binary formats used by pre-2007 versions of Office. The filter provides extensive configuration options organized by document type (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) plus general options that apply across all formats.

inlineCodes.description: Inline code detection and handling settings. When enabled, uses regex-based pattern matching to detect inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.) within translatable text and represent them as protected codes. Includes options for merging adjacent codes, moving boundary codes to skeleton, and simplifying code representation.

word.translateHeadersFooters.description: When enabled, exposes header and footer content in Word documents for translation.
notes: Default: on

word.translateHidden.description: When enabled, exposes hidden text in Word documents for translation.
notes: Default: on (per wiki; schema default is false)

word.translateComments.description: When enabled, exposes document comments for translation. This is part of the General Options in the UI and applies to Word documents.
notes: Default: on

word.translateDocProperties.description: When enabled, exposes the following document properties for translation: title, subject, creator, description, category, keywords, content status.
notes: Default: on

word.excludeGraphicMetaData.description: When enabled, excludes graphic metadata from extraction in Word documents.
notes: Default: off

word.excludeColors.description: When enabled, excludes text using specific font colors from translation. Text with colors matching any selected color values will not be exposed for translation.
notes: Default: none selected

word.allowStyleOptimisation.description: When enabled, allows style optimization — common formatting of all runs in a paragraph is moved to the styles part of the document.
notes: Default: on

word.translateNumberingLevelText.description: When enabled, exposes numbering-level text for translation in Word documents.
notes: Default: off

word.ignoreFontColors.description: When enabled, font colors will be ignored during processing. If both 'Clean Tags Aggressively' and this option are checked and the ignorance thresholds are empty, the font color run properties are removed from the document structure on filtering, meaning font color information will also be absent on merge.
notes: Default: off

If aggressiveCleanup and this option are both enabled with empty thresholds, font colour run properties are removed from the document structure entirely — this means font colour information is also absent on merge.

dependsOn.condition: When both are enabled with empty thresholds, font color run properties are removed from the document structure

word.fontColorsMin.description: When defined, font colors will be ignored starting from the specified value. Can be empty (considered as white color by default), and can contain preset color values or RGB hex strings. Examples: black, Black, 000000 — these represent thresholds in white.
notes: Default: empty (none)
Accepts preset color names (e.g. 'black', 'Black') or RGB hex strings (e.g. '000000')

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

word.fontColorsMax.description: When defined, font colors will be ignored ending at the specified value. Can be empty (considered as white color by default), and can contain preset color values or RGB hex strings. Examples: white, White, FFFFFF — these represent thresholds in white.
notes: Default: empty (none)
Accepts preset color names (e.g. 'white', 'White') or RGB hex strings (e.g. 'FFFFFF')

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

word.automaticallyAcceptRevisions.description: When enabled, tracked changes (revisions) in Word documents are automatically accepted before processing.
notes: Default: on

excel.translateHidden.description: When enabled, hidden rows and columns in Excel spreadsheets are exposed for translation.
notes: Default: off

excel.translateSheetNames.description: When enabled, extracts sheet names in Excel workbooks for translation.
notes: Default: off

excel.translateDrawings.description: When enabled, extracts text from shapes and drawings in Excel spreadsheets for translation.
notes: Default: off

excel.translateDiagramData.description: When enabled, exposes diagram data in Excel spreadsheets for translation.
notes: Default: off

excel.excludeColors.description: Text with a foreground or background color matching any of the selected colors will be excluded from translation. The named colors available in the UI correspond to the standard color palette of Excel 2010. The configuration also supports colors specified as RGB in the format RRGGBB, so specific colors not explicitly listed in the UI may be excluded by modifying the .fprm file by hand.
notes: Default: none
To exclude custom RGB colors (e.g. #69b3e7 / Pantone 292), modify the tsExcelExcludedColors section of the configuration file directly: tsExcelExcludedColors.i=1 and ccc0=69b3e7

excel.preserveStylesInTargetColumns.description: When enabled, the cell styles in target columns are preserved during translation merge.
notes: Default: off

excel.translateCellsCopied.description: When enabled, cell data are copied on extraction to allow contextualized and independent translations.
notes: Default: on

extractExcelSourceAndTargetColumnsJoined.description: When enabled, the source and target columns (cells in a row) are joined on extraction.
notes: Default: off

extractExcelWorksheetsExplicitlySpecifie.description: When enabled, only worksheets that match their names in the Worksheet Configurations are exposed for extraction.
notes: Default: off

extractExcelCellsExplicitlySpecified.description: When enabled, only cells specified in the Worksheet Configurations are exposed for extraction. The explicitly mentioned source and target columns are eligible for such handling.
notes: Default: off

treatExcelSourceColumnStylesForExclusion.description: When enabled, source column styles are treated for exclusion in Excel processing.
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.translateHidden.description: When enabled, hidden slides in PowerPoint presentations are exposed for translation.
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.translateMasters.description: When enabled, exposes slide masters and notes masters for translation. This will also expose for translation content from layouts that are currently in use by at least one slide.
notes: Default: on

powerpoint.translateNotes.description: When enabled, slide notes are exposed for translation in PowerPoint presentations.
notes: Default: off (per wiki; schema default is true)

powerpoint.includedSlideNumbersOnly.description: When enabled, only specific slide numbers are included for translation.
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.ignorePlaceholdersInMasters.description: When enabled, placeholders in slide masters are ignored and not exposed for translation.
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.reorderNotes.description: When enabled, the note parts are reordered and placed after the related slide part and after its chart parts.
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.reorderCharts.description: When enabled, the chart parts are reordered and placed after the related slide or layout or master part and after their diagram data parts.
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.reorderComments.description: When enabled, the comment parts are reordered and placed after the related slide part and after its note parts.
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.reorderDiagramData.description: When enabled, the diagram data parts are reordered and placed after the related slide or layout or master part and after their relationship parts.
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.reorderDocProperties.description: When enabled, the document properties are reordered and placed after the root relationship part (_rels/.rels).
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.reorderRelationships.description: When enabled, the relationship parts are reordered and placed after the related slide or layout or master part.
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.reorderNotesAndComments.description: When enabled, reorders both notes and comments together in the output.
notes: Default: off

powerpoint.translateCharts.description: When enabled, chart text in PowerPoint presentations is exposed for translation.
notes: Default: on

powerpoint.translateComments.description: When enabled and the same option is checked under the General Options (they will be separated after the next release), the document comments are exposed for translation in PowerPoint.
notes: Default: on
Currently coupled with the General Options 'Translate Comments' setting — both must be enabled. They will be separated in a future release.

dependsOn.condition: Currently must also be enabled (shared General Options toggle; will be separated in a future release)

powerpoint.translateDiagramData.description: When enabled, diagram data in PowerPoint presentations is exposed for translation.
notes: Default: on

powerpoint.translateDocProperties.description: When enabled and the same option is checked under the General Options (they will be separated after the next release), the following document properties are exposed for translation: title, subject, creator, description, category, keywords, content status.
notes: Default: on
Currently coupled with the General Options 'Translate Document Properties' setting — both must be enabled. They will be separated in a future release.

dependsOn.condition: Currently must also be enabled (shared General Options toggle; will be separated in a future release)

translateWordGraphicName.description: When enabled, @name attribute values associated with drawings and word art in Word documents are exposed for translation.
notes: Default: on

translateWordGraphicDescription.description: When enabled, @descr attribute values associated with drawings and word art in Word documents are exposed for translation.
notes: Default: off

translatePowerpointGraphicName.description: When enabled, @name attribute values associated with drawings and word art in PowerPoint presentations are exposed for translation.
notes: Default: on

translatePowerpointGraphicDescription.description: When enabled, @descr attribute values associated with drawings and word art in PowerPoint presentations are exposed for translation.
notes: Default: off

translatePowerpointCachedChartStrings.description: When enabled, the cached chart strings in PowerPoint presentations are exposed for translation.
notes: Default: off

translatePowerpointCachedChartNumbers.description: When enabled, the cached chart numbers and format codes in PowerPoint presentations are exposed for translation.
notes: Default: off

removeEmbeddedExcel.description: When enabled and either cached chart strings or numbers are also set for extraction, the embedded Excel package is removed, and any references to it in chart parts and related relationships are removed as well.
notes: Default: off

dependsOn.condition: At least one of translatePowerpointCachedChartStrings or translatePowerpointCachedChartNumbers must be enabled for this option to have effect
At least one of translatePowerpointCachedChartStrings or translatePowerpointCachedChartNumbers must be enabled for this option to have effect

general.extractExternalHyperlinks.description: When enabled, external hyperlinks in the document are extracted for translation.
notes: Default: off

general.inExcludeMode.description: Controls the include/exclude mode for styles. When set to 'Include', only text using selected styles will be extracted for translation. When set to 'Exclude', all content except text using selected styles will be extracted for translation.
notes: Default: on (Exclude mode)

general.inExcludeHighlightMode.description: Controls the highlight-based include/exclude mode. When set to 'Include', only text in the specified highlight colors will be extracted for translation. When set to 'Exclude', all content except text in the specified highlight colors will be extracted. The default is Exclude with no colors selected, meaning all visible content is extracted.
notes: Default: on (Exclude mode with no colors selected)
Text excluded using this mechanism will be treated as hidden; the 'Translate Hidden' options will still extract it.
Starting in 1.48.0, this option also applies to content in PowerPoint files.

general.addLineSeparator.description: When enabled, adds a line separator as a character in the extracted content.
notes: Default: off

general.addTabAsCharacter.description: When enabled, adds tab characters as actual characters in the extracted content rather than treating them as formatting.
notes: Default: off

general.aggressiveCleanup.description: When enabled, strips additional formatting tags related to text spacing. This is meant to improve filtering in cases where Office documents were converted from other formats (in particular, PDF), and imperfect conversion added a lot of extra formatting noise.
notes: Default: off

general.allowEmptyTargets.description: When enabled, allows empty target segments in the translated output.
notes: Default: off

general.ignoreSoftHyphenTag.description: When enabled, soft hyphen tags in the document are ignored during processing.
notes: Default: off

general.replaceNoBreakHyphenTag.description: When enabled, no-break hyphen tags are replaced during processing.
notes: Default: off

general.lineSeparatorReplacement.description: Specifies the replacement string used for line separators in extracted content.
notes: Default: newline character (\n)

general.maxAttributeSize.description: Maximum size in bytes for XML attributes in the OpenXML document structure.
notes: Default: 4194304 (4 MB)

general.fileType.description: Specifies the type of file being processed. Valid values: MSWORD, MSEXCEL, MSWORDDOCPROPERTIES, MSPOWERPOINTCOMMENTS.
notes: Default: MSWORD

ignoreWhitespaceStyles.description: When enabled under the 'Clean Tags Aggressively' option, the whitespace character styles (formatting) are ignored and considered equal to the consequential ones.
notes: Default: off

dependsOn.condition: Only takes effect when aggressiveCleanup (Clean Tags Aggressively) is enabled

preserveAsciiAndHighAnsiFontCategoriesOn.description: When enabled, the ASCII and HighAnsi run font categories are preserved on the merge of consequential runs.
notes: Default: off

limitations: Various limitations exist; see the Okapi Framework Bitbucket issues list filtered for OpenXML.
