Parallel Corpus Files strip all inline codes and produce plain text with one entry per line; locale codes are appended to filenames.
Word Table output represents inline codes in generic format; when input is TTX, match percentage is displayed in the Type column.
Documents generated by this step are not meant for round-trip merging back into original format.

examples.title: Convert to TMX with single output
examples.description: Generates a single TMX file from all input documents, outputting only approved entries with text content.

examples.title: Convert to PO with auto extensions
examples.description: Generates one PO file per input document, with output paths derived from input paths plus the .po extension.

examples.title: Convert to Pensieve TM with overwrite
examples.description: Outputs to a Pensieve TM directory, replacing existing entries when the source text matches.

full-width-conversion.overview: This step converts characters in text units from or to full-width form (zenkaku). For historical reasons, some Asian character sets have two display forms for some characters: half-width and full-width. The modification is done in the text of the text units for the specified target locale; if there is no text for the specified target, the source text is copied to the target and processed. Takes filter events and sends filter events.

toHalfWidth.description: When true, converts all full-width characters to their half-width or ASCII equivalents. For example, 'Ｑ' (U+FF31) is converted to 'Q' (U+0051) and 'サ' (U+30B5) is converted to 'ｻ' (U+FF7B). When false, converts in the opposite direction: all half-width and ASCII characters are converted to their full-width equivalents. For example, 'Q' (U+0051) is converted to 'Ｑ' (U+FF31) and 'ｻ' (U+FF7B) is converted to 'サ' (U+30B5).

asciiOnly.description: When set, converts only ASCII characters to full-width. Half-width characters are left as half-width. This option applies only when converting to full-width (toHalfWidth is false).

dependsOn.condition: must be false (converting to full-width)

katakanaOnly.description: When set, converts only Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation (｡､｢｣, etc.) into their full-width forms. This is a separate option to facilitate normalizing modern Japanese text: half-width Katakana should be converted to full-width while alphanumeric characters should remain half-width. This option applies only when converting to full-width (toHalfWidth is false).

dependsOn.condition: must be false (converting to full-width)

includeSLA.description: When set, also converts the Squared Latin Abbreviations of the CJK Compatibility block into sequences of non-CJK characters. For example, '㏀' (U+33C0) is converted to "kΩ" (U+006B, U+03A9). This option applies when converting to half-width.

dependsOn.condition: must be true (converting to half-width)

includeLLS.description: When set, also converts several characters of the Letter-Like Symbols block to character sequences. Conversions include: U+2100→a/c, U+2101→a/s, U+2105→c/o, U+2103→°C, U+2109→°F, U+2116→No, U+212A→K, U+212B→Å. This option applies when converting to half-width.

dependsOn.condition: must be true (converting to half-width)

includeKatakana.description: When set, converts Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation (。、「」, etc.) into their half-width forms. This is a separate option (and off by default) to facilitate normalizing modern Japanese text: full-width alphanumeric characters may need to be normalized to half-width while Katakana should remain full-width. This option applies when converting to half-width.

dependsOn.condition: must be true (converting to half-width)

normalizeOutput.description: When enabled, applies Unicode NFC normalization to the output text if any conversions are made. Converting half-width forms to full-width can result in decomposed forms — for instance, ﾌﾟ (U+FF8C U+FF9F) → プ (U+30D5 U+309A). Normalization ensures the standard composed representation is used: プ (U+30D7).

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: The modification is done on the text of the text units for the specified target locale. If there is no text for the specified target, the source text is copied to the target and then processed.
Converting half-width forms to full-width can result in Unicode decomposed forms; enable normalizeOutput to ensure NFC-normalized output.

examples.title: Normalize Japanese text (half-width alphanumeric, full-width Katakana)
examples.description: Convert full-width alphanumeric characters to half-width while leaving Katakana in full-width form. This is a common normalization for modern Japanese text.
examples.input: toHalfWidth: true, includeKatakana: false (default), normalizeOutput: true
examples.output: Full-width ASCII/alphanumeric characters like Ｑ (U+FF31) are converted to Q (U+0051), while Katakana characters like サ (U+30B5) remain unchanged.

examples.title: Convert half-width Katakana to full-width only
examples.description: Convert half-width Katakana to full-width while leaving alphanumeric characters in their current form. Useful for normalizing Japanese text where Katakana should be full-width but ASCII should stay half-width.
examples.input: toHalfWidth: false, katakanaOnly: true, normalizeOutput: true
examples.output: Half-width Katakana like ｻ (U+FF7B) is converted to サ (U+30B5). ASCII characters remain unchanged. NFC normalization is applied to handle decomposed forms.

examples.title: Full-width to half-width with Letter-Like Symbols
examples.description: Convert full-width characters to half-width and also expand Letter-Like Symbols into character sequences.
examples.output: Full-width characters are converted to half-width equivalents, and Letter-Like Symbols such as ℃ (U+2103) are expanded to °C.

id-based-aligner.overview: This step aligns the text units of two input files based on matching ids. The ids are taken from the name (TextUnit.getName()) of each text unit. Any filter that produces unique names for its text units will work with this aligner, for example the Properties Filter. Takes filter events and sends filter events. When TMX generation is disabled, each returned text unit is a new aligned bilingual text unit; when enabled, events are returned unchanged and a TMX file is produced.

generateTMX.description: When set to true, the step produces a TMX file and the events returned by the step are unchanged. When set to false, each text unit in the returned events is a new text unit with the aligned source and target content.
notes: Note: for target <tuv> data to be generated in the TMX, the 'Copy to/over the target' option (copyToTarget) must also be checked.

tmxOutputPath.description: Full path of the output TMX file to be generated.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this parameter to take effect

replaceWithSource.description: If no target text is available for a given text unit, use the source text as fallback.

copyToTarget.description: Copy the aligned target content to/over the target of the text unit. Existing target will be lost, and the copied target will not be segmented. If the entry from the 'target' file is set to approved, the approved property is passed along too. An annotation will still be created if requested via storeAsAltTranslation.
notes: WARNING: Copied target will not be segmented and any existing target will be lost.
Note: This option must also be checked for target <tuv> data to be generated when generateTMX is true.

storeAsAltTranslation.description: Store the matched target as an alternate translation annotation attached to the processed entry, so that subsequent steps in the pipeline can see and use it.

suppressTusWithNoTarget.description: When enabled, prevents the step from passing on any text units for which no target content could be found during alignment.

useTextUnitIds.description: Align based on TextUnit IDs rather than resource names (TextUnit.getName()). When false, alignment uses the name property of each text unit.

limitations: Assumes that each text unit has a unique name value. The filter being used must produce unique names (TextUnit.getName()) for all text units in the documents.
This step aligns text units only, not the possible segments inside the text units.
processingNotes: The process expects both inputs to be non-segmented.
The 'source' file is the first input and provides source content. The 'target' file is the second input and provides target content.

If the 'target' file is in a monolingual format (like Java properties), the source extracted from that file is used as target content. If the 'target' file is multilingual (like XLIFF), the target extracted from that file is used as target content.

If the 'target' file is multilingual, in addition to matching on id, the step verifies that the source content of both text units with the same name have the same source text. If they do not match, no alignment is made.
Text units in the source but not in the target generate a warning. Text units in the target but not in the source are ignored.

id-based-copy.overview: This step copies text from a reference file (second input) into a destination file (first input) for text units that share the same id (based on TextUnit.getName()). It works with any filter that produces unique names for its text units, such as the Properties Filter. The reference file is read into an in-memory table indexed by text unit name, and for each text unit in the destination file, a matching entry is looked up and its content copied into the target. Takes filter events and sends filter events.

markAsTranslateNo.description: When a text unit has a match in the reference file, setting this option marks the text unit as non-translatable in the destination file. Entries that do not have a match in the reference file have this property left unchanged. Only applies to matched text units.
notes: Entries in the destination file that are already set to non-translatable are not modified regardless of this setting.

markAsApproved.description: When a text unit has a match in the reference file, setting this option sets the target property 'approved' to 'yes' on the matched text unit in the destination file. Entries that do not have a match in the reference file have this property left as it was.

limitations: Uses an in-memory table for the reference file, so very large reference files may cause memory limitations.
No adjustment is made for inline codes when copying content from the reference file into the destination file — results may vary depending on file formats and whether inline codes are present.
The process copies complete text unit content with no adjustment for segmented entries. Use the Desegmentation Step beforehand if needed.
Both files must have text units with unique names (e.g., unique resname in XLIFF).
processingNotes: The reference file (second input) is read entirely into an in-memory table indexed on text unit name before the destination file is processed.

If the reference file is monolingual (e.g., properties file), the source text of matched text units is copied into the target. If the reference file is multilingual (e.g., PO file), the target text is used instead.
Entries in the destination file that are set to non-translatable are never modified, regardless of parameter settings.
After processing each pair of input files, entries in the reference table that were not used are listed as warnings.
No warning is generated if an entry in the destination file is not found in the reference file.
If a destination file is not associated with a reference file, a warning is generated and the file is treated as having an empty reference file.
When working with lists of input files, they should be paired — the first file in Input List 1 must correspond to the first file in Input List 2, and so forth.
The entries in the reference and destination files do not have to be sorted.

examples.title: Copying properties translations into a destination file
examples.description: Using two properties files where text units share the same key names. The reference file contains translated values and the destination file contains the original keys. The step matches entries by their property key (text unit name) and copies the reference source text into the destination target. markAsApproved is set to true (default) so matched entries are marked as approved, and markAsTranslateNo is false (default) so entries remain translatable.
examples.input:
Destination file (Input List 1): app_en.properties
Reference file (Input List 2): app_fr.properties
markAsApproved: true
markAsTranslateNo: false
examples.output: Matched text units in the destination file receive the French text as their target, with the approved property set to yes.

image-modification.overview: This step allows you to modify the size or the format of an image. It takes a raw document as input and sends back a raw document. It can scale image dimensions by percentage and convert between image formats or to grayscale.

scaleWidth.description: Set the percentage of the width for the new image, compared to the original. Must be greater than 0. Use 100 for the same width as the original.

scaleHeight.description: Set the percentage of the height for the new image, compared to the original. Must be greater than 0. Use 100 for the same height as the original.

makeGray.description: Set this option to convert the image to an image where colors are converted into a set of grays.

format.description: Select the format for the output images. Use an empty string (same as the original) to create an output in the same format as the original. If the original format is not supported for output, a warning is generated and the output is done in PNG if possible.
notes: If you select a specific format, the extension is not changed automatically.

limitations: The image formats supported on input varies depending on your Java VM, version and platform.
processingNotes: If the original image format is not supported for output, a warning is generated and the output falls back to PNG if possible.
Selecting a specific output format does not automatically change the file extension.

inconsistency-check.overview: Generates a report of potential inconsistencies found by comparing source and target segments across multiple input documents. It checks for inconsistencies by comparing the target of each unique source and the source of each unique target. The XML report lists all potential inconsistencies by their "key text" with corresponding source or target strings for easy review. Takes filter events and sends filter events.

checkInconststencies.description: Set this option to perform the check for inconsistencies. This option allows you to disable the step in a given pipeline without removing it.

checkPerFile.description: Set this option to process each input file individually rather than comparing across all input documents. When enabled, inconsistencies are checked on a file-by-file basis instead of across the entire batch.
notes: Consider selecting this option if there are issues with memory usage.

outputPath.description: The full path of the XML report file to generate. You can use the variable ${rootDir} or ${inputRootDir} in the path.

displayOption.description: Controls how the report displays inline codes. The choices are: 'original' — display inline codes as they appear in the input documents; 'generic' — display inline codes as generic markers (e.g., <1></1> or <2/>); 'plaintext' — remove all inline codes from the report.

autoOpen.description: Set this option to automatically open the generated XML report file after the process is complete.

limitations: You may run out of memory when processing a large number of segments.
Inline codes are not taken into account when doing comparisons.
The comparison is case-sensitive and whitespace-sensitive.
Text units or segments that have no target are not included in the check.
Text units or segments that are empty are included in the check.
processingNotes: Inline codes are not taken into account when doing comparisons.
The comparison is case-sensitive and whitespace-sensitive.
Text units or segments that have no target are not included in the check.
Text units or segments that are empty are included in the check.
The step compares both directions: target of each unique source, and source of each unique target.
The generated report is in XML format and lists inconsistencies grouped by their key text.

leveraging.overview: This step leverages existing translations into extracted text units by querying translation resources (TM engines or MT systems) and applying matching translations. It processes filter events, sending text units or segments to a configured translation resource connector and attaching matching translations as annotations or copying them directly into the target content. Non-translatable and approved text units are skipped. Multiple leveraging steps can be chained to query several resources in sequence (e.g., TM first, then MT for unleveraged entries).

leverage.description: Enables or disables the leveraging step. When set to false, the step is effectively bypassed. This option allows you to keep the step in your pipeline and toggle it on and off without removing it.

resourceClassName.description: The fully qualified Java class name of the translation resource connector to use. It can be a TM or an MT engine; the available connectors may vary based on your installation. A summary of the current options for the selected resource is displayed in the UI. Internet-based resources may be slow and result in lengthy processing time. For more information see the Connectors documentation.
notes: Internet-based resources may be slow and result in lengthy processing time.

noQueryThreshold.description: The no-query threshold score. If an entry already has a candidate with a score equal to or above this value, no query is performed for it. This allows you to skip for-fee translation resources if the entry already has a good enough candidate. Use 101 to always allow the query to be done (effectively disabling this optimization).

threshold.description: The minimum match score threshold. Matches with a score below this value are not taken into account. Matches equal to or above this score are placed into annotations attached to the target container or target segments of the text unit.

fillTarget.description: When enabled, fills the target with the best translation candidate if its score is equal to or above the fillTargetThreshold value. The match is still added to the list of potential matches regardless of this setting.

fillTargetThreshold.description: The threshold score equal to or above which the best match is copied into the target content. If the score of the match is lower than this threshold, the match is not copied into the target content but is still added to the list of potential matches for the given text unit.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

fillIfTargetIsEmpty.description: When enabled, the best match translation is only copied into the target if the target is currently empty, preventing overwriting of existing translations.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

fillIfTargetIsSameAsSource.description: When enabled, overwrites target content that is the same (text and codes) as the source with the leveraged translation. This option is only enabled when fillIfTargetIsEmpty is also set.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

downgradeIdenticalBestMatches.description: When enabled, downgrades by 1% the score of top exact matches that are identical. Some connectors may return a set of exact matches that are identical except for their translation. Because some components may trigger automated processes when the best match is an exact match, setting this option ensures that nothing is triggered automatically when there are several identical best matches.

makeTMX.description: When enabled, creates a TMX document containing all entries for which a match was found. A single TMX document is created for all input files. The output path is specified by the tmxPath parameter.

tmxPath.description: The full path for the generated TMX document. Supports the variables ${rootDir} and ${inputRootDir}, as well as source or target locale variables (${srcLoc}, ${trgLoc}, etc.).

dependsOn.condition: must be true

useMTPrefix.description: When enabled, adds an MT-specific prefix to the source text if the entry is coming from an MT engine. This ensures the entry will only be used as a fuzzy match in tools that cannot identify the origin of the entry as MT using the creationid attribute.

useTargetPrefix.description: When enabled, adds the specified prefix (targetPrefix) at the front of the translated text when a match is found and copied into the target, depending on its score relative to targetPrefixThreshold.

targetPrefix.description: The prefix string to add at the front of leveraged translations when useTargetPrefix is enabled and the match score is equal to or below targetPrefixThreshold.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

targetPrefixThreshold.description: The threshold score equal to or below which the prefix is added at the front of the leveraged translation. Only applies when useTargetPrefix is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

copySourceOnNoText.description: When enabled, copies the content of the source entry into the target if the source has no text (i.e., it contains only white spaces and/or codes). The copy overwrites any existing target content.

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: Text units flagged as non-translatable are skipped.
Text units flagged as approved (the 'approved' property is set to 'yes') are skipped.
Matches are placed into annotations attached to the target container or target segments of the text unit.
Multiple leveraging steps can be chained to leverage against several translation resources in sequence (e.g., TM first, then MT for unleveraged entries).
A single TMX document is created for all input files when TMX generation is enabled.

line-break-conversion.overview: The Line-Break Conversion step converts all line-breaks in an input document to a specified type (DOS/Windows, Unix/Linux, or Macintosh). It takes a raw document as input and sends back a raw document. The type of line-break in the input file is detected automatically, and the file may contain a mix of different types of line-breaks.

lineBreak.description: Select the new type of line-break for the output files. The value is an encoded string representing the desired line-break characters. Valid choices are: DOS/Windows (Carriage Return + Line-Feed, \r\n, 0x0D+0x0A) represented as '$0d$$0a$'; Unix/Linux (Line-Feed, \n, 0x0A) represented as '$0a$'; Macintosh (Carriage-Return, \r, 0x0D) represented as '$0d$'. The default is '$0a$' (Unix/Linux).
notes: The input line-break type is detected automatically and the file may contain a mix of different types

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: You must specify the input encoding of the document, even for formats where it is usually automatically detected (like XML, HTML, etc). The only exception is files with a Byte-Order-Mark.
The output is produced in the same encoding as the input document.
Byte-Order-Marks are preserved if they are present in the input.
The type of line-break in the input file is detected automatically. The file may contain a mix of different types of line-breaks.

examples.title: Convert to Unix line endings
examples.description: Convert a file with mixed or Windows line-breaks to Unix/Linux line-feed (\n) format. Set lineBreak to '$0a$' (the default value).
examples.output: All line-breaks in the output file will be \n (0x0A)

examples.title: Convert to Windows line endings
examples.description: Convert a file to DOS/Windows CR+LF line-break format. Set lineBreak to '$0d$$0a$'.
examples.output: All line-breaks in the output file will be \r\n (0x0D+0x0A)

examples.title: Convert to classic Mac line endings
examples.description: Convert a file to Macintosh carriage-return format. Set lineBreak to '$0d$'.
examples.output: All line-breaks in the output file will be \r (0x0D)

localizable-checker.overview: This step checks that all localizables (dates, times, and numbers) exist in the target and are localized correctly. It compares source and target number, date, and time patterns to assure they are properly localized for the target locale. The step takes filter events as input and sends filter events as output, adding issue annotations if a target localizable is missing or not properly localized.
limitations: Numbers that use different units in the source and target will be flagged as false positives (e.g., imperial vs. metric and currency conversions).
processingNotes: The step checks source and target for dates, times (in various formats), and numbers.
Issues are reported as annotations on the filter events when a target localizable is missing or not properly localized for the locale.
The step has no user-configurable parameters; all checking behavior is automatic based on source and target locales.
Requires both source and target locale to be set via parameter mappings (SOURCE_LOCALE, TARGET_LOCALE).

m-s-batch-translation.overview: This step annotates text units of input documents with Microsoft Translator candidates or/and creates a TM from them. It takes filter events as input and sends back filter events (possibly annotated) or a raw document. Unlike using the Leveraging Step with the Microsoft Translator Connector, this step can process several text units at once and is therefore much faster. Text units flagged as non-translatable are not sent for translation.

azureKey.description: Microsoft Azure subscription key used to authenticate with the Microsoft Translator v3 API. If you already have a key for version 2, the same key should work. For information on how to obtain an azure key, see the Azure Cognitive Services pricing page.
notes: The v2 API was retired on 2019-04-30. You must use the v3 API with an Azure key from M38 onwards.
If you already have a key for version 2, the same key should work for version 3.

category.description: An optional category to use when working with trained engines. You can either enter directly the engine identifier (called 'category' in Microsoft Translator Hub), or you can use a keyword in the form @@@keyword@@@. If you specify a keyword you must specify a properties file in the Engine Mapping (configPath) field. The keyword can be a literal string or the ${domain} variable. When ${domain} is used, the variable is replaced by the first occurrence of the value for the ITS Domain annotation found on a text unit. Ideally this Domain annotation should be set on the first text unit of the first document processed. All batches of events translated before a domain annotation is found are translated with the empty category.
notes: Only the first occurrence of the Domain annotation has an effect on the selection of the engine.

Because this step works on batches, segments before the first occurrence of the Domain annotation but within the same batch will be translated with the engine specified by the domain. For example: If you have 100 events and the Events buffer is set to 50 and the first occurrence of the Domain annotation is in the 60th event: The first 50 events will be translated with the empty category and all the other events with the engine corresponding to the specified domain, including the events 51 to 59.

dependsOn.condition: must be set when using @@@keyword@@@ syntax in category

tmxPath.description: Full path of the new TMX document to create. If another document exists already it will be overwritten. You can use the variables ${rootDir} and ${inputRootDir}, as well as any of the source or target locale variables (${srcLoc}, ${trgLoc}, etc).

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this path to be used

configPath.description: Full path of the properties file that contains the mapping between category keywords and Microsoft Translator Hub engine identifiers. You can use the variables ${rootDir} and ${inputRootDir}, as well as any of the source or target locale variables (${srcLoc}, ${trgLoc}, etc). Leave the path empty to not use a mapping. The properties file is a list of lines in the form: <keyword>.<language>=<engineID>, where <keyword> is a case-sensitive string (without spaces, equal signs, or periods) corresponding to the keyword part in @@@keyword@@@, and <language> is the uppercase language code of the target locale. There is a fallback mechanism: if specifying @@@client2.law@@@ it would first look for client2.law.DE, and if not found would look for client2.DE. If no custom engine is found, the generic Microsoft-provided engine is used.

dependsOn.condition: must use @@@keyword@@@ syntax for this mapping to take effect

markAsMT.description: Set this option to mark the TM entries generated as the result of machine translation. For example, when this option is set, the creationId attribute of the TMX <Tu> element is set to "MT!".

maxEvents.description: The number of events to buffer for a single query to the engine. The larger the buffer, the faster the processing. However, there are limitations related to the volume of text you can process at once as well.

maxMatches.description: The maximum number of matches allowed per source text.

threshold.description: The score below which a match is not kept as a result. See the Microsoft Translator Connector documentation to understand how scores are computed based on their match degree and rating values.

makeTmx.description: Set this option to create a TMX output. When enabled, specify the full path of the TMX document to generate in the tmxPath parameter.

annotate.description: Set this option to add annotations to the text units that hold the matches found. Those annotations may be used later by other steps. Existing annotations are preserved.

fillTarget.description: Set this option to copy the translation with the best score (and a score above or equal to the Fill threshold) into the target if it is empty. Only the matches returned by the Microsoft Translator engine are taken into account.

dependsOn.condition: used to determine the minimum score for filling the target

fillTargetThreshold.description: If the score of the best match is below this value, no translation is copied into the target. Only applies when fillTarget is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to take effect

onlyWhenWithoutCandidate.description: Set this option to send to Microsoft Translator only the text for which there is currently no candidate (i.e., annotations added by previous steps or coming from the original document).

sendTmx.description: Set this option to have the generated TMX document as the only input raw document passed on to the next step of the pipeline. If this option is not set, the filter events are passed on to the next step instead.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for a TMX document to exist to send

useTargetPrefix.description: Set this option to add a prefix to the translation candidate copied in the target. The prefix text is specified by the targetPrefix parameter.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for the prefix to be applied

targetPrefix.description: Text to use as the prefix added to translation candidates copied into the target.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this prefix to be used

limitations: The Microsoft Translator API has some restrictions for high volume usage. Contact Microsoft for details.
Only the first ITS Domain annotation in a batch is taken into account.
The v3 API no longer supports translation memory functionality, so that aspect of the step is not available.
The M38 implementation of v3 API support is minimal and does not support new features such as profanity filtering.
See also the limitations on the Microsoft Translator Connector.
processingNotes: Text units flagged as non-translatable are not sent for translation.
Events are buffered according to maxEvents before being sent as a batch query to the engine. Larger buffers result in faster processing but are subject to API volume limitations.
When using ${domain} variable in the category field, the domain is resolved from the first ITS Domain annotation encountered. All events in batches before that annotation use the empty category.
The v2 API (Client ID / Client Secret authentication) was retired on 2019-04-30. From M38 onward, use the Azure subscription key with the v3 API.
Improving MT output can be done with post-processing steps such as the Space Check Step to fix extra or missing spaces around inline codes.

examples.title: Engine mapping properties file
examples.description: Example properties file for mapping category keywords to Microsoft Translator Hub engine identifiers. Keywords are case-sensitive and language codes are uppercase. A fallback mechanism tries progressively shorter keyword prefixes.
examples.output: Use @@@travel@@@ in Category to select the first engine (for French target). Use @@@client1.tech@@@ to select the third engine. @@@client2.law@@@ would first look for client2.law.DE, fall back to client2.DE, or use the generic engine if neither is found.

merging.overview: This step merges the files of a package created by the Rainbow Translation Kit Creation Step back into their original format. It reads a manifest file (manifest.rkm) using the Rainbow Translation Kit Filter and recreates translated files in their original formats. Takes filter events as input and sends filter events or raw documents depending on options.

preserveWhitespace.description: When enabled, preserves whitespace in the merged output. This option controls whether whitespace formatting from the original document is maintained during the merging process.

preserveSegmentation.description: Set this option to preserve the segmentation in the text unit events sent to the step after this one. When merging a text unit, the content normally needs to be un-segmented. To save processing time, the segmentation is not restored by default because this step is most often used as the last step of the pipeline, making the segmentation unnecessary. Set this option to make sure the segmentation is restored when subsequent steps need it.
notes: Segmentation is not restored by default to save processing time, since this step is most often used as the last step of the pipeline.

returnRawDocument.description: Set this option to return raw document events rather than filter events. If you have steps after this one, you may want to use either the current filter events that are being processed, or a raw document event for each of the output merged file generated by this step. Set this option to send raw document events.

forceTargetLocale.description: Set this option to force the target locale to be taken from the tool calling the step rather than from the manifest file. When disabled, the target locale specified in the manifest is used.

overrideOutputPath.description: Enter the directory where you want the output to be generated. Leave this field empty to use the output path defined in the manifest. This allows overriding the default output location specified in the manifest.rkm file.

copyCodeMetadata.description: When enabled, copies the code metadata from the original file during the merging process. This ensures that inline code metadata from the source document is preserved in the merged output.

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: The input file must be the manifest.rkm file of the translation package.
The translated XLIFF documents must be in the 'work' folder of the package.
The original documents must be in the 'original' folder of the package.
The options in each manifest file can also be edited when you start processing it — see the Rainbow Translation Kit Manifest page for more details.
Segmentation is not restored by default to save processing time, since this step is typically the last in the pipeline.

examples.title: Simplest merge pipeline
examples.description: The simplest pipeline to merge the files of a translation kit uses Raw Document to Filter Events Step followed by the Rainbow Translation Kit Merging Step. The input file is the manifest.rkm file of the package.
examples.input:
Pipeline:
= Raw Document to Filter Events Step
+ Rainbow Translation Kit Merging Step

Input: manifest.rkm file from the translation package
examples.output: Translated files in their original formats, generated in the output location specified by the manifest (or overridden by the overrideOutputPath parameter).

paragraph-aligner.overview: This step aligns paragraphs (TextUnits) from two documents. It takes filter events as input and sends only TextUnit filter events as output — all other events are lost. The ResourceSimplifier is called internally to flatten out events and expand TextUnits so that all TextUnits are available for alignment and skeleton is available for diffing if the option is enabled.

outputOneToOneMatchesOnly.description: When enabled, only 1-to-1 paragraph aligned matches are output. Paragraphs that could not be cleanly aligned in a one-to-one correspondence are excluded from the output. This is enabled by default.

useSkeletonAlignment.description: When enabled, skeleton alignment is used to provide better alignment anchor points. This is an experimental feature that may improve alignments for some formats by leveraging skeleton (non-translatable) content as additional reference points during the alignment process. Disabled by default.
notes: This is an experimental feature.

limitations: Standard Gale and Church alignment is not accurate for long runs of paragraphs.
The experimental 'Use Skeleton Alignment' option may improve alignments for some formats but is not guaranteed to help in all cases.
processingNotes: The ResourceSimplifier is called internally to flatten out events and expand TextUnits so that all TextUnits are available for alignment and skeleton is available for diffing.
Only TextUnit events are passed along — all other event types (document parts, groups, sub-documents, etc.) are lost in the output.
This step sends a special PIPELINE_PARAMETERS event that informs subsequent steps (e.g., Sentence Aligner) that the target input has been consumed and is no longer available.
Requires both a source and target document as input, along with source and target locale settings and a filter configuration mapper.

post-segmentation-code-simplifier.overview: This step simplifies inline codes by trimming and merging them within linguistically distinct segments of a TextUnit. It must be run after segmentation. It joins adjacent inline codes inside segments and optionally moves leading and trailing codes from the segment to inter-segment TextParts. Original un-merged codes are preserved as okp:merged attributes in generated XLIFF output, and trimmed codes are written outside the "mrk" elements. Takes filter events and sends filter events.

removeLeadingTrailingCodes.description: When enabled, removes leading and trailing inline codes from the text units and places them outside the segment. In practice, this moves leading and trailing codes from the source to inter-segment TextParts. Trimmed codes are written outside the 'mrk' elements in the generated output. Enabled by default.

mergeCodes.description: When enabled, merges adjacent inline codes within the text units into a single code. Original un-merged codes are saved as okp:merged attributes inside the generated XLIFF file. Enabled by default.

limitations: Bi-lingual formats such as XLIFF, TMX, TTX etc. will not have their codes simplified, as the codes may differ in source and target. Codes must align with IDs across source and target.
processingNotes: This step must be run after segmentation — it operates on linguistically distinct segments within a TextUnit.
Original un-merged codes are preserved as okp:merged attributes inside the generated XLIFF file.
Trimmed codes are written outside the 'mrk' elements in the output.

quality-check.overview: The Quality Check step generates a report of potential issues found by comparing the source and target of text units. It accepts filter events or raw documents and sends them through to the next step unchanged. When processing filter events, the quality check report is generated at the end of the batch; when processing raw documents, a CheckMate session is opened for interactive issue browsing. No check is done on entries set as non-translatable.

leadingWS.description: Set this option to flag text units where the leading white spaces are different between source and target. Detects whitespace inconsistencies at the start of segments.

trailingWS.description: Set this option to flag text units where the trailing white spaces are different between source and target. Detects whitespace inconsistencies at the end of segments.

emptyTarget.description: Set this option to flag segments for which the translation is empty when the corresponding source is not empty. Only applies when the source contains content.

emptySource.description: Set this option to flag segments for which the target is not empty while its source is empty. Detects unintended target-only content.

targetSameAsSource.description: Set this option to flag segments where the translation is the same as the source. This check is done only if the source segment contains in its text at least one word-character (a character included in the regular expression: [\p{Ll}\p{Lu}\p{Lt}\p{Lo}\p{Nd}]). Inline codes are not part of the text comparison. When a target is found to be the same as its source, the tool checks the list of patterns that have their expected target set to '<same>'. If the string matches one of those patterns, no warning is generated.
notes: For example, '%s : %d' would not be checked (no word-characters), but '-------- S= %d' would be checked.
Inline codes are not part of the text of the entry.

targetSameAsSourceForSameLanguage.description: Set this option to enable the 'target same as source' verification even when the language of the source and target locale codes are the same (same language family). For example, this enables checking when source is 'en' and target is 'en-GB'.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to have any effect

targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.description: Set this option to include inline codes in the comparison when verifying if the target is the same as the source. If this option is set and the only difference between the source and the translation is an inline code, the segment will not be flagged.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to have any effect

targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.description: Set this option to include numbers in the comparison when verifying if the target is the same as the source. When enabled, segments that differ only in numeric values will not be flagged.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to have any effect

doubledWord.description: Set this option to flag target segments where there is a sequential repetition of the same word, for example 'is is' in 'this is is an example'. The check is not case-sensitive, so 'This this an example' would also be flagged.

doubledWordExceptions.description: Enter the list of words that can be repeated without triggering a doubled-word warning. Each word must be separated by semi-colons with no spaces around the semi-colon. The exceptions are not case-sensitive. For example, in French some sentences may have 'vous vous' or 'nous nous' — enter 'vous;nous' to allow these.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for exceptions to be relevant

checkXliffSchema.description: Set this option to validate XLIFF documents against an XLIFF schema. A document is detected as XLIFF if it is associated with a filter configuration starting with 'okf_xliff'.

xliffSchemaType.description: Select the type of XLIFF schema to validate against. Default is 'Transitional' (xliff-core-1.2-transitional.xsd).

dependsOn.condition: must be true for schema type to be used

includeTextContainers.description: Set this option to include text containers (non-segment text content) in the quality checks, in addition to segment-level content.

useGenericCodes.description: Set this option to use generic code representations when comparing inline codes between source and target, rather than the original code content.

codeDifference.description: Set this option to verify that the target content has the same inline codes as the source content. This compares the content of the codes between source and target when content is available; otherwise the codes' type and id are compared. Both missing codes (in source but not target) and extra codes (in target but not source) are indicated. A difference only in the order of the codes does not trigger a warning. If no extra or missing codes are found, the program checks for possible issues in the sequence of opening/closing codes — groups can be moved, and change of parent as well as switch of closing and opening sequence are reported.

guessOpenClose.description: Set this option to let the program try to detect whether placeholder codes are really opening or closing codes for XML formats. If an opening/closing type is detected, it is included in the verification of the opening/closing sequence.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to have any effect

strictCodeOrder.description: Set this option to require that inline codes appear in exactly the same order in the target as in the source. By default (false), a difference only in the order of codes does not trigger a warning.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to have any effect

typesToIgnore.description: Enter the list of all the types of inline codes the verification should ignore, separated by semi-colons. For example, '<mrk>' elements in XLIFF or '<df>' elements in TTX. Default value includes 'mrk' and 'x-df-s'.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for ignored types to be relevant

extraCodesAllowed.description: Number of codes allowed to be extra in the target (present in the translation but not in the source). The strings are case-sensitive. Set to 0 to disallow any extra codes.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this setting to be relevant

missingCodesAllowed.description: Number of codes allowed to be missing from the target (present in the source but not in the translation). The strings are case-sensitive. Set to 0 to disallow any missing codes.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this setting to be relevant

checkPatterns.description: Set this option to verify that each source pattern defined in the pattern list has its corresponding expected part in the target content. Patterns are defined as regular expressions with source/target pairs.

patternCount.description: The number of pattern entries defined. Each pattern entry consists of a set of properties indexed from 0 to patternCount-1: usePatternN, fromSourcePatternN, singlePatternN, severityPatternN, sourcePatternN, targetPatternN, and descPatternN.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for patterns to be checked

showOnlyPatternDescription.description: Set this option to show only the pattern description in the report output instead of the full pattern details.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to be relevant

usePattern0.description: Set whether this pattern entry is active. Uncheck to disable the pattern without removing it.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for patterns to be checked

fromSourcePattern0.description: If true (Src indicator), the source pattern is looked at first — if found, the corresponding pattern is searched in the target. If false (Trg indicator), the target pattern is looked at first, then searched in the source. Setting to false allows detecting extra patterns in the target.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this pattern to be active

singlePattern0.description: If true, the pattern is treated as a single occurrence check rather than a count-based comparison between source and target.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this pattern to be active

severityPattern0.description: The severity level for this pattern's warning. Values: 0 = low, 1 = medium, 2 = high.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this pattern to be active

sourcePattern0.description: The regular expression of the pattern to look for in the source text. This is a Java-compatible regular expression.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this pattern to be active

targetPattern0.description: The target pattern corresponding to the part found in the source. If the part should be the same as in the source, use the '<same>' keyword.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this pattern to be active

descPattern0.description: A short description of this pattern rule, displayed in the report output.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this pattern to be active

dependsOn.condition: must be true
