corruptedCharacters.description: Set this option to check for special patterns that often indicate a file with corrupted characters — for example, a UTF-8 file opened as ISO-8859-1. This feature does not find all possible cases of corrupted characters, only some of the frequent ones.

checkCharacters.description: Set this option to check the characters of the target text against a given character set encoding specified in 'charset'. You can configure only a character set encoding, only a regular expression of allowed characters (extraCharsAllowed), or both.

charset.description: Enter the name of a valid character set encoding to check against, such as 'ISO-8859-1'. You can also leave this field empty to use only the list of allowed characters provided in extraCharsAllowed.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for charset checking to be active

extraCharsAllowed.description: Optionally enter a regular expression that matches a list of allowed characters. Characters matching this expression will be allowed even if they are not part of the character set encoding specified in 'charset'. Leave empty to not use any additional character allowance.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this to be relevant

checkAllowedCharacters.description: Set this option to enable checking of allowed characters in the target text. Works in conjunction with charset and extraCharsAllowed settings.

checkWithLT.description: Set this option to run the verifications provided by a LanguageTool server. You must have access to a LanguageTool Checker running as a server (most commonly a local server).
notes: Using LanguageTool may increase significantly the processing time.
Using the auto-translate option increases the processing time further.

serverURL.description: Enter the URL of the LanguageTool server to use for verification. The default is 'http://localhost:8081/' for a local LanguageTool server instance.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for the server URL to be used

ltBilingualMode.description: Set this option to use the 'bitext' mode, where the source text of the segment is also sent to LanguageTool. This may reduce the number of false warnings.
notes: This option does not work with older versions of LanguageTool.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for bilingual mode to be used

checkTerms.description: Set this option to verify translation terminology. The given glossary file is read and for each segment, the tool searches for existing terms in the source, then searches in the target text for the corresponding translation. If no corresponding translation is found, a warning is issued.
notes: Warning: The Terms feature is under development.

termsPath.description: Enter the full path of the glossary file to use. Supported formats: TBX (TermBase eXchange format), CSV (comma-separated values, must be UTF-8, first column is source terms, second is translation), and tab-delimited (must be UTF-8, first column is source terms, second is translation). Files with .tbx extension are imported as TBX, .csv as CSV, and .txt or any other extension as tab-delimited. Other columns are ignored. CSV values with commas must be enclosed in double-quotes; literal double-quotes must be escaped as two double-quotes.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for terminology verification to be active

stringMode.description: Set this option to use the terms as full strings for complete match verification. When enabled, the tool looks for complete matches rather than partial term matches. This can be used to verify UI strings in a document, for example.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for string mode to be relevant

betweenCodes.description: Set this option to require that strings match only if they are surrounded by inline codes. Used in combination with stringMode for stricter matching.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to be relevant

checkBlacklist.description: Set this option to detect usage of blacklisted terms. The specified blacklist file is read and for each segment, the target text is searched for any blacklisted term. If a blacklisted term is found, a warning is issued.

blacklistPath.description: Enter the full path of the blacklist file to use. The file must be in UTF-8. Column 1 contains the blacklisted term, column 2 is an optional replacement suggestion, column 3 (M35+) can contain a comment, and column 4 (M35+) can contain an integer indicating severity (0, 1, or 2, with 0/low as default). Other columns are ignored.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for blacklist checking to be active

allowBlacklistSub.description: Set this option to allow blacklist substitution suggestions to be included in the warning output when a blacklisted term is found.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to be relevant

blacklistSrc.description: Set this option to also check the source text for blacklisted terms, in addition to the target text.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to be relevant

scope.description: Determines which entries to process. Value 0 = process all entries, value 1 = process only approved entries (Approved property set to 'yes'), value 2 = process only non-approved entries (no Approved property or not set to 'yes'). The Approved property setting is specific to each file format.
notes: Entries flagged as non-translatable are never processed, regardless of the scope setting.

outputPath.description: Enter the full path of the report file to generate. You can use the variable '${rootDir}' in the path. Default is '${rootDir}/qa-report.html'.

outputType.description: Select the format of the report output. Value 0 = HTML file, value 1 = tab-delimited file (UTF-8, columns: location, message, source text, target text), value 2 = XML document.

autoOpen.description: Set this option to automatically open the report file after the process is complete.

showFullPath.description: Set this option to show the full path of documents with issues in the report. If not set, the report shows relative paths with the longest common root directories removed.

saveSession.description: Set this option to save the quality check session when the step is run. This is only available when editing parameters for a step (not in CheckMate standalone).
notes: This option is available only when editing the parameters for a step.

sessionPath.description: Enter the full path of the session file to save. You can use the variable '${rootDir}' in the path. Default is '${rootDir}/qa-session.qcs'.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for session to be saved

checkStorageSize.description: Set this option to check the byte length of text units that have an ITS Storage Size metadata property (its-storageSize).

checkMaxCharLength.description: Set this option to flag any target text that is longer than a given percentage of its source text character length. Allows setting different percentages for short versus long text. The length is based on the number of characters without counting inline codes.
notes: These values must be tuned for each source/target language pair.

maxCharLengthBreak.description: Enter the number of characters above which text is considered 'long' (vs. 'short') for maximum length checking. This threshold determines which percentage (maxCharLengthAbove or maxCharLengthBelow) applies.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to be used

maxCharLengthAbove.description: Enter the maximum allowed percentage of source length for 'short' text (text with character count at or below the maxCharLengthBreak threshold). For example, 200 means the target can be at most 200% of the source length.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this percentage to be used

maxCharLengthBelow.description: Enter the maximum allowed percentage of source length for 'long' text (text with character count above the maxCharLengthBreak threshold). For example, 350 means the target can be at most 350% of the source length.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this percentage to be used

checkMinCharLength.description: Set this option to flag any target text that is shorter than a given percentage of its source text character length. Allows setting different percentages for short versus long text. The length is based on the number of characters without counting inline codes.
notes: These values must be tuned for each source/target language pair.

minCharLengthBreak.description: Enter the number of characters above which text is considered 'long' (vs. 'short') for minimum length checking. This threshold determines which percentage (minCharLengthAbove or minCharLengthBelow) applies.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to be used

minCharLengthAbove.description: Enter the minimum allowed percentage of source length for 'short' text (text with character count at or below the minCharLengthBreak threshold). For example, 45 means the target must be at least 45% of the source length.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this percentage to be used

minCharLengthBelow.description: Enter the minimum allowed percentage of source length for 'long' text (text with character count above the minCharLengthBreak threshold). For example, 30 means the target must be at least 30% of the source length.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this percentage to be used

checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.description: Set this option to flag any target text that is longer than an absolute maximum number of characters, regardless of the source length.

absoluteMaxCharLength.description: Enter the absolute maximum number of characters allowed in the target text. Any target exceeding this length will be flagged.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this limit to be enforced

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: No check is done on entries set as non-translatable, regardless of scope settings.
With filter events input: events are processed and sent to the next step; the quality check report is generated at the end of the batch of input files. No interactive UI is provided.
With raw documents input: documents are added to a new quality check session and sent to the next step. At the end of the batch, the session is opened in CheckMate for interactive issue browsing.
Character length calculations are based on the number of characters without counting inline codes.
Length percentage thresholds must be tuned for each source/target language pair.
Using LanguageTool may increase processing time significantly.
The corrupted characters check does not find all possible cases, only some frequent ones.
The Terms feature is under development.

examples.title: Default pattern: Opening parenthesis
examples.description: Default pattern 0 checks that the count of opening parentheses matches between source and target, including both ASCII and fullwidth forms.

examples.title: Same-content pattern with <same> keyword
examples.description: Patterns for content that must be identical in source and target (e.g., email addresses, URLs, IP addresses) use the '<same>' keyword as the target pattern.
examples.input:
sourcePattern3: [\w\.\-]+@[\w\.\-]+
targetPattern3: <same>
descPattern3: Email addresses
severityPattern3: 1 (medium)

examples.title: Target-only pattern check (tripled letter)
examples.description: Pattern 7 demonstrates a target-only check (fromSourcePattern7: false) that looks for tripled letters in the target text, which often indicates a typo.
examples.input:
fromSourcePattern7: false
sourcePattern7: <same>
targetPattern7: ([\p{Ll}\p{Lu}\p{Lt}\p{Lo}])\1\1
descPattern7: Tripled letter
severityPattern7: 1 (medium)

examples.title: Doubled word exceptions for French
examples.description: Configure doubled-word checking to allow legitimate repeated words in French like 'vous vous' and 'nous nous'.

examples.title: Length check with short/long thresholds
examples.description: Configure maximum length checking with different percentages for short and long text segments. Text up to 20 characters uses a 200% threshold, while longer text uses 350%.

r-t-f-conversion.overview: This step removes the RTF layer from input files, outputting only the visible text. It takes raw documents and sends back raw documents. Only visible text is output — hidden or deleted text, images, and other special content is discarded, as is any formatting beyond spaces, line-breaks, and tabs. It can be used to post-process Trados-Tagged RTF files, outputting only the translated sections.

bomOnUTF8.description: When enabled, adds a Byte-Order-Mark (BOM) at the beginning of the output file if the output encoding is UTF-8. The BOM is a special Unicode character used to signal the byte order of a text stream. Only applies when the output encoding is UTF-8. For more information on the BOM see the Unicode FAQ on UTF BOM.

updateEncoding.description: When enabled, automatically updates the encoding declaration in the output file to match the selected output encoding. This option works for XML and HTML files. In XML files, the encoding attribute of the XML declaration is updated, or added if not present. If the file has no XML declaration, nothing is updated. In HTML files, the step looks for the pattern 'content=... charset=...' and when found, sets the charset value to the output encoding. If the pattern is not found, nothing is updated.
notes: The automatic update of encoding declarations for XML and HTML works based on pattern matching, not by parsing the files, and therefore is not perfectly accurate. For example, a commented-out declaration could be updated.

lineBreak.description: Selects the type of line-break to use in the output files. Valid choices are: DOS/Windows (Carriage Return + Line-Feed, \r\n, 0x0D+0x0A), Unix/Linux (Line-Feed, \n, 0x0A), or Macintosh (Carriage-Return, \r, 0x0D). The default value '$0a$' corresponds to Unix/Linux line-feed.

limitations: Characters encoded as SYMBOL fields are not currently output. These include drawing symbols inserted from Word's menu using fonts such as Dingbats or Wingdings.

The automatic update of encoding declarations for XML and HTML works based on pattern matching, not by parsing the files, and therefore is not perfectly accurate. For example, a commented-out declaration could be updated.
processingNotes: Only visible text of the RTF is output; hidden or deleted text, images, and other special content is discarded.
Any formatting done other than using spaces, line-breaks, and tabs is discarded.
A warning is issued for each line where one or more characters cannot be represented in the selected output encoding.

Can be used to post-process Trados-Tagged RTF files — only the translated sections are output. Alternatively, the Trados-Tagged RTF Filter can be used to access both source and target text with inline codes.

raw-document-to-filter-events.filterName: Raw Document to Filter Events Step
raw-document-to-filter-events.overview: This step extracts the content of a raw document into a set of filter events. It takes a raw document with an associated filter configuration and sends the filter events down the pipeline. This step is typically the first one of the pipeline, but it can be placed after any step that sends a raw document. To convert filter events back to a raw document, use the Filter Events to Raw Document Step.
limitations: None known.
processingNotes: The step requires an associated filter configuration on the raw document to produce filter events.
A simple extract-then-merge pipeline can be constructed by combining this step with the Filter Events to Raw Document Step.

examples.title: Simple extract and merge pipeline
examples.description: A minimal pipeline that extracts a document into filter events and then merges them back into a raw document.
examples.input: Raw Document to Filter Events Step → Filter Events to Raw Document Step

remove-target.overview: The Remove Target step removes target entries from text units in a filter events pipeline. It can remove all targets across all text units, or selectively remove targets based on text unit IDs or target locales. The step takes filter events as input and sends filter events as output.

tusForTargetRemoval.description: A comma-delimited list of text unit IDs specifying which text units should have their targets removed. When this field is empty, all targets on all text units are removed. Only used when filterBasedOnIds is true.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this parameter to be used as the filter criterion

targetLocalesToKeep.description: A comma-delimited list of locale codes identifying which target locales should be kept (not removed). Targets for locales not in this list will be removed. When this field is empty, all targets are kept (none are removed based on locale). Only used when filterBasedOnIds is false.

dependsOn.condition: must be false for this parameter to be used as the filter criterion

filterBasedOnIds.description: Controls the filtering mode for target removal. When true, the step filters text units based on the IDs specified in tusForTargetRemoval. When false, the step filters based on the locales specified in targetLocalesToKeep. You cannot filter on both IDs and locales simultaneously — only one mode is active at a time.
notes: You cannot filter on both IDs and locales simultaneously; this boolean selects which mode is active.

removeTUIfNoTarget.description: Controls whether a text unit should be completely removed from the event stream if it has no remaining targets after the removal operation. When true, text units left with no targets are removed entirely. When false, text units with no remaining targets are passed through unchanged.

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: All target languages are removed by default when no specific text unit IDs are provided.
The step operates on filter events, processing each text unit individually as it passes through the pipeline.

examples.title: Remove all targets from all text units
examples.description: Leave the tusForTargetRemoval field empty and set filterBasedOnIds to true. This removes every target from every text unit in the input.
examples.output: All text units have their targets removed.

examples.title: Remove targets from specific text units by ID
examples.description: Specify a comma-delimited list of text unit IDs to selectively remove targets only from those text units.
examples.output: Only text units with IDs tu1, tu2, and tu5 have their targets removed.

repetition-analysis.overview: This step analyzes repetitions in input documents, performing either exact or configurable fuzzy matching. It creates RepetitiveSegmentAnnotation (attached to repetitive source segments) and AltTranslationsAnnotation (attached to corresponding target segments) for found repetitive segments. Takes filter events and sends filter events.
limitations: None known.
processingNotes: AltTranslationsAnnotation is not attached for the first repetitive segment in its group, to avoid it being double-counted by counting steps as repetitive with itself.
Two annotation types are created: RepetitiveSegmentAnnotation on the source segment, and AltTranslationsAnnotation on the target segment corresponding to a repetitive source.

resource-simplifier.overview: The Resource Simplifier step simplifies the resources of filter events by splitting the generic skeleton of a given event resource into parts that contain no references. Skeleton parts are attached to newly created DOCUMENT_PART events, original references are converted to skeleton parts or TEXT_UNIT events, and the resulting sequence is packed into a single MULTI_EVENT event. It takes filter events as input and sends filter events as output.
limitations: None known.
processingNotes: Skeleton parts are attached to newly created DOCUMENT_PART events.
Original references are converted either to skeleton parts or TEXT_UNIT events.
The sequence of DOCUMENT_PART and TEXT_UNIT events is packed into a single MULTI_EVENT event.
Simplification algorithm is format-specific.

scoping-report.overview: The Scoping Report Step creates a template-based report on various counts (word count, character count, etc.) and optionally leveraged data. It takes filter events as input and sends filter events as output. To include leveraging statistics, the pipeline must include prior steps that leverage translations (such as the Leveraging Step). For just word- or character-count annotations without a report, use the Word Count Step or Character Count Step instead.

projectName.description: The name of the project to be displayed in the title of the report. This value populates the PROJECT_NAME report field in the generated output.

customTemplateURI.description: URI or full path of a custom template to be used to generate the report. Templates support plain text and HTML formats and contain text with report field placeholders enclosed in brackets. If this field is left empty, or if the specified URI is not found, the default HTML template is used. See the Templates section for the template format.
notes: If the custom template field is left empty or the specified URI is not found, the default template is used.

customTemplateString.description: A string for directly specifying the report template content inline, rather than pointing to a file via customTemplateURI. Templates contain text and report field placeholders enclosed in brackets. Table rows are enclosed in brackets around a row of column fields.

outputPath.description: Full path of the report file to generate. You can use the ${rootDir} variable, as well as source or target locale variables (${srcLoc}, ${trgLoc}, etc.).

countAsNonTranslatable_GMXProtected.description: When enabled, words/characters in the PROJECT_GMX_PROTECTED_WORD_COUNT and ITEM_GMX_PROTECTED_WORD_COUNT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent text marked as 'protected' or otherwise not translatable (e.g., XLIFF text enclosed in <mrk mtype="protected"> elements).

countAsNonTranslatable_GMXExactMatched.description: When enabled, words/characters in the PROJECT_GMX_EXACT_MATCHED_WORD_COUNT and ITEM_GMX_EXACT_MATCHED_WORD_COUNT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent text units matched unambiguously with a prior translation requiring no translator input. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_GMXLeveragedMatch.description: When enabled, words/characters in the PROJECT_GMX_LEVERAGED_MATCHED_WORD_COUNT and ITEM_GMX_LEVERAGED_MATCHED_WORD_COUNT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent text units matched against a leveraged translation memory database. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_GMXRepetitionMatc.description: When enabled, words/characters in the PROJECT_GMX_REPETITION_MATCHED_WORD_COUNT and ITEM_GMX_REPETITION_MATCHED_WORD_COUNT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent repeating text units not matched in any other form. Repetition matching takes precedence over fuzzy matching. Typically provided by the Repetition Analysis Step.
notes: Repetition matching is deemed to take precedence over fuzzy matching.

countAsNonTranslatable_GMXFuzzyMatch.description: When enabled, words/characters in the PROJECT_GMX_FUZZY_MATCHED_WORD_COUNT and ITEM_GMX_FUZZY_MATCHED_WORD_COUNT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent text units fuzzy matched against a leveraged translation memory database. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_GMXAlphanumericOn.description: When enabled, words/characters in the PROJECT_GMX_ALPHANUMERIC_ONLY_TEXT_UNIT_WORD_COUNT and ITEM_GMX_ALPHANUMERIC_ONLY_TEXT_UNIT_WORD_COUNT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent text units identified as containing only alphanumeric words.

countAsNonTranslatable_GMXNumericOnlyTex.description: When enabled, words/characters in the PROJECT_GMX_NUMERIC_ONLY_TEXT_UNIT_WORD_COUNT and ITEM_GMX_NUMERIC_ONLY_TEXT_UNIT_WORD_COUNT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent text units identified as containing only numeric words.

countAsNonTranslatable_GMXMeasurementOnl.description: When enabled, words/characters in the PROJECT_GMX_MEASUREMENT_ONLY_TEXT_UNIT_WORD_COUNT and ITEM_GMX_MEASUREMENT_ONLY_TEXT_UNIT_WORD_COUNT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent word counts from measurement-only text units.

countAsNonTranslatable_ExactUniqueIdMatc.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_EXACT_UNIQUE_ID and ITEM_EXACT_UNIQUE_ID categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match EXACT and also match a unique id. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_ExactPreviousVers.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_EXACT_PREVIOUS_VERSION and ITEM_EXACT_PREVIOUS_VERSION categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match EXACT and come from the preceding version of the same document (e.g., if v4 is leveraged, this match must come from v3, not v2 or v1). Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_ExactLocalContext.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_EXACT_LOCAL_CONTEXT and ITEM_EXACT_LOCAL_CONTEXT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match EXACT and also match a small number of segments before and/or after. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_ExactDocumentCont.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_EXACT_DOCUMENT_CONTEXT and ITEM_EXACT_DOCUMENT_CONTEXT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match EXACT and come from the same document. Typically provided by the Repetition Analysis Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_ExactStructuralMa.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_EXACT_STRUCTURAL and ITEM_EXACT_STRUCTURAL categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match EXACT and also match the structural type of the segment (title, paragraph, list element, etc.). Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_ExactMatch.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_EXACT and ITEM_EXACT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match text and codes exactly. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_ExactTextOnlyPrev.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_EXACT_TEXT_ONLY_PREVIOUS_VERSION and ITEM_EXACT_TEXT_ONLY_PREVIOUS_VERSION categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match EXACT_TEXT_ONLY and come from a previous version of the same document. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_ExactTextOnlyUniq.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_EXACT_TEXT_ONLY_UNIQUE_ID and ITEM_EXACT_TEXT_ONLY_UNIQUE_ID categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match EXACT_TEXT_ONLY and also match a unique id. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_ExactTextOnly.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_EXACT_TEXT_ONLY and ITEM_EXACT_TEXT_ONLY categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match text exactly, but have a difference in one or more codes. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_ExactRepaired.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_EXACT_REPAIRED and ITEM_EXACT_REPAIRED categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match text and codes exactly, but only after some automated repair (e.g., number replacement, code repair, capitalization, punctuation, etc.). Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_FuzzyPreviousVers.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_FUZZY_PREVIOUS_VERSION and ITEM_FUZZY_PREVIOUS_VERSION categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match FUZZY and come from a previous version of the same document. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_FuzzyUniqueIdMatc.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_FUZZY_UNIQUE_ID and ITEM_FUZZY_UNIQUE_ID categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match FUZZY and also match a unique id. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_FuzzyMatch.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_FUZZY and ITEM_FUZZY categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match both text and/or codes partially. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_FuzzyRepaired.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_FUZZY_REPAIRED and ITEM_FUZZY_REPAIRED categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent segments that match both text and/or codes partially and some automated repair (e.g., number replacement, code repair, capitalization, punctuation, etc.) was applied to the target. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_PhraseAssembled.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_PHRASE_ASSEMBLED and ITEM_PHRASE_ASSEMBLED categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent matches assembled from phrases in the TM or other resources (different algorithms could be used).

countAsNonTranslatable_MT.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_MT and ITEM_MT categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent translations coming from a machine translation engine. Typically provided by the Leveraging Step.

countAsNonTranslatable_Concordance.description: When enabled, words in the PROJECT_CONCORDANCE and ITEM_CONCORDANCE categories are counted as non-translatable. These represent TM concordance or phrase matches (usually a word or term only).

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: The Scoping Report Step is primarily a presentation layer — it displays information provided by other steps. If required upstream steps (e.g., Word Count Step, Character Count Step, Leveraging Step) are not included in the pipeline, their corresponding report fields will show zeros.

To have leveraging statistics, the pipeline must include prior steps that leverage translations, such as the Leveraging Step. Some filters like the XLIFF Filter may also generate resources with leveraged data.
Character count report fields exclude whitespace and punctuation characters. Separate fields exist for whitespace and punctuation counts.
The countAsNonTranslatable_* parameters determine which match categories are subtracted from the total to compute translatable vs. non-translatable word/character counts.

examples.title: CSV-style plain text template
examples.description: A custom template that produces a CSV report with per-item leveraging breakdown and project totals. Report fields are enclosed in brackets, and table rows use double-bracket notation [[...]] to iterate over items.
examples.input:
Project Name: [PROJECT_NAME]
Creation Date: [PROJECT_DATE]
Target Locale: [PROJECT_TARGET_LOCALE]

File,Exact Previous Version Matches,Exact Local Context Matches,100% Matches,Fuzzy Matches,Repetitions,Total,
[[ITEM_NAME],[ITEM_EXACT_PREVIOUS_VERSION],[ITEM_EXACT_LOCAL_CONTEXT],[ITEM_EXACT],[ITEM_FUZZY],[ITEM_GMX_REPETITION_MATCHED_WORD_COUNT],[ITEM_TOTAL_WORD_COUNT],]
Total,[PROJECT_EXACT_PREVIOUS_VERSION],[PROJECT_EXACT_LOCAL_CONTEXT],[PROJECT_EXACT],[PROJECT_FUZZY],[PROJECT_GMX_REPETITION_MATCHED_WORD_COUNT],[PROJECT_TOTAL_WORD_COUNT]
examples.output:

W3CHTMHLTest1.html,10,23,12,57,12,54,
